text:
ok so me and my boyfriend were literally just driving around looking for somewhere to eat bc we couldn't decide on anything (classic us lol) and we ended up just pulling in here on a whim and honestly?? so glad we did

the banchan they bring out at the start is SO good like we were already filling up on that before our actual food even came out lmao. i got the bibimbap and he got the bulgogi and both were really solid, nothing crazy mind blowing but like genuinely good comfort food you know? the portions are pretty generous too which i appreciate bc some korean places around here are kinda skimpy

the vibe inside is nothing special tbh, pretty basic decor, but it was clean and the service was friendly enough. our server was a little hard to flag down at times but she was sweet when she did come over so no big deal

only reason im not giving 5 stars is the wait was kinda long for a tuesday night like we waited almost 25 mins which i wasnt expecting?? and the tofu soup my friend had last time she came was apparently way too salty so idk maybe inconsistent on some dishes

but overall we'll def be back, already talking about trying the korean fried chicken next time. good solid spot for austin korean food 👍

ok so i really wanted to like this place bc everyone keeps hyping it up but honestly?? super disappointed. waited like 45 mins for a table which fine whatever but then the food took FOREVER and when it finally came out my burger was literally cold in the middle like how does that even happen. the fries were soggy too not even a little crispy. our server was nice enough i guess but she disappeared for like 20 mins at one point and we couldnt get refills or anything. and for LA prices i expected way better tbh we spent like $80 for two people and left hungry lol. the vibe is cute i'll give em that, nice lighting and stuff but that doesnt make up for mid food and slow service. probably wont be coming back anytime soon

ok so like honestly this place is fine?? the bibimbap was pretty good ngl but we waited like 45 mins for a table on a friday which like ok i get it its popular but they didnt even tell us how long the wait would be we just kind of stood there like idiots lol. the banchan was good, loved the kimchi but one of the little dishes had something in it that tasted kinda off idk what it was. service was nice enough our server was sweet but she disappeared for like forever when we needed refills. the portions are decent for the price i guess. i'd probably come back but like not on a weekend, the vibe gets too hectic and loud and u can barely hear the person across from u. its not bad its just not amazingg either ya know

ok so me and my girls have been looking for a good mediterranean spot in austin for FOREVER and we finally found it omg. came here last saturday after trying to figure out where to eat for like 45 minutes lol and honestly so glad we ended up here

the hummus?? insane. like actually the best ive had outside of my aunts house and she would probably be offended if she knew i said that lol. we got the lamb kebabs and the falafel plate to share and everything was so fresh tasting, you could tell they actually care about the food

our server was super chill and patient with us bc we had a million questions about the menu (my friend doesnt eat pork so we were double checking everything) and he was really helpful about it

the vibe inside is really cute too, nice lighting, not too loud so we could actually have a conversation which is rare for austin restaurants tbh

only thing is parking was kind of a pain but thats like every restaurant here so whatever

prices were totally reasonable for the quality, we all split the check and nobody was mad about it lmao. already planning to come back and bring my boyfriend who is obsessed with mediterranean food. he's gonna lose his mind over the pita bread alone

highly recommend 10/10

ok so i literally came here on a whim bc my usual spot (pesce on west 6th) had like a 2 hour wait and omg best accident ever?? the shrimp tacos here are INSANE like i dont even know how to describe it the sauce they put on there is so good i asked the waiter what was in it and he just smiled lol so i guess its a secret

the vibe is super chill too, not trying too hard like some of the other seafood places downtown that feel like ur eating in a museum or something. sat outside which was a lil hot tbh but thats austin in july what do u expect

got the crawfish bisque as a starter and honestly it wrecked me in the best way possible, so creamy and rich but not heavy?? idk how they do that. my bf got the grilled redfish and he literally did not speak for like 10 min bc he was so into it haha

prices are totally reasonable too like we both ate and had drinks and it wasnt painful at all. service was fast and our girl checked on us the right amount, not too much not too little

def coming back and bringing my whole friend group, already texted the group chat lol. 10/10 no notes

ok so i came here w my boyfriend last saturday and honestly?? it was pretty solid. the burger was LEGIT like one of the better ones ive had in LA and thats saying something bc this city has a million burger spots lol. fries were crispy which is literally all i ask for in life tbh

service was like fine, our server was nice enough but def took a while to get our drinks refilled, not a huge deal tho. the vibe inside is cool, kinda loud but in a fun way u know? good for like a date or hanging w friends

only reason im not giving 5 stars is the prices are a liiittle steep for what u get like $18 for a burger is that really necessary?? but i mean its LA so what do i expect lol. also my boyfriends mac n cheese was just ok, nothing special

but overall would def come back, probably gonna try the chicken sandwich next time bc i saw someone elses plate and it looked amazing. parking was actually not terrible which like in this neighborhood is honestly a miracle in itself lmaooo

ok so i really wanted to love this place bc everyone keeps talking abt it but honestly?? major disappointment. we waited like 45 mins for a table which fine whatever its miami but then the food took FOREVER and when it finally came out the shrimp were rubbery and tasted like they'd been sitting out for a while. my bf got the snapper and said it was just ok nothing special. for the prices they charge you'd think they could at least make sure the seafood is fresh like thats literally the whole point?? the server was nice enough i guess but kept disappearing and we had to ask someone else for water twice. the vibe is cute tho ill give em that, very instagrammable lol. but looks dont make up for mediocre food imo. prob wont be back there are too many good spots in miami to settle for this

ok so i've been coming here like every other week since my coworker dragged me here back in march and honestly i cannot stop thinking about their birria tacos?? like i grew up in san antonio so i KNOW what good mexican food is supposed to taste like and this place actually gets it right which is so rare in nashville lol

the consomé had me literally doing a little happy dance at the table my bf was embarrassed but whatever it was worth it. chips and salsa come out super fast and the salsa has actual heat to it not that watery mild stuff

ok and can we talk about the margaritas bc wow. got the mango one last time and it was so good i had two and probably shouldnt have on a tuesday but here we are lmao

i used to always go to mas tacos on porter when i wanted a quick fix but honestly this place has kind of taken over that spot in my rotation, mas tacos is still great dont get me wrong but the sit down vibe here and the full menu options just hit different when you actually want like a real meal

service has always been friendly and pretty quick even when it looks packed. parking can be a lil annoying just a heads up but its totally worth the extra like 5 min of circling around

just go already seriously

ok so honestly this place is really good?? like we came here on a random tuesday and the pasta was SO fresh, you could tell. service was a lil slow but whatever the vibe made up for it. the tiramisu omg. def coming back with my whole family lol. only docking a star bc parking is a nightmare

ok so like i've been coming here for a few months now and honestly?? it's pretty solid for miami. the burgers are THICK like actually juicy not those sad flat ones you get at half these places. fries were crispy which like.. shouldn't be a big deal but somehow is lol. only reason im not giving 5 stars is the wait time is kinda ridiculous, we sat there for like 40 mins on a tuesday night which makes zero sense to me. also our server forgot my bf's drink twice which was a lil annoying tbh. but the food when it finally came out was genuinely really good, the mac n cheese side is insane btw definitely get that. prices are fair for the area too which is saying something bc miami will rob you blind lmaooo. would def go back tho

ok so i've been to a LOT of bbq spots in austin and honestly this place holds up pretty well?? like the brisket was super tender, had that perfect smoke ring and the bark was like.. chef's kiss. got the fatty cut obv bc why would u ever get lean lol

the sides were good too, mac and cheese was creamy not that dry stuff u sometimes get, and the pickles and onions they put out were fresh which i appreciate. line moved faster than i expected for a saturday which was a nice surprise tbh

only reason im not giving 5 stars is the sausage link was a little underwhelming compared to everything else, like it had good flavor but the casing was kinda tough and i've had better around town. also the seating situation is a bit chaotic but like its austin bbq what do u expect lol

staff was super friendly tho, the guy at the counter gave me an extra slice to try which was so nice and totally unprompted. would def come back, probably will drag my coworkers here next time they visit from out of town bc honestly what better way to show someone austin than a place like this

solid solid spot, just a couple small things keeping it from being perfect

ok so i literally dont even know where to start lol. came here last saturday with my boyfriend and his sister and honestly it was just a disaster from the beginning. we waited like 25 mins to even get seated even tho there were empty tables everywhere?? the guy at the front (i think his name was raj or something idk he had a blue shirt on) was just on his phone the whole time not even acknowledging us

anyway we finally sit down and order the butter chicken and some samosas to start. the samosas came out COLD like not even a little warm, completely cold in the middle. and the butter chicken omg it tasted like they just opened a jar of tomato sauce and threw some chicken in it?? my boyfriend makes better butter chicken at home and hes not even indian lol

we also got the garlic naan which was the only ok thing tbh but even that was kinda soggy on one side

asked for water refills twice and got ignored both times, had to like flag someone down. and the prices are NOT cheap for this quality like i paid $18 for that butter chicken which was genuinely inedible

left most of our food on the table and nobody even asked if everything was ok. never coming back, theres way better indian food in LA for way less money

ok so me and my boyfriend were looking for somewhere to eat before catching a show at the opera house and we ended up here bc the photos online looked SO good like the food looked amazing and the vibe seemed cool. big mistake lol

honestly where do i even start. we waited like 25 mins just to get water and the place wasnt even that busy?? our server seemed annoyed that we exsisted which is always a great sign. i ordered the lamb kebabs and my bf got the falafel plate and like... the falafel was SO dry, he kept reaching for water after every bite. mine wasnt terrible but it was pretty bland for mediterranean food like theres supposed to be flavor yknow?? 

the pita bread was the best part and thats kind of sad

also the prices are NOT cheap so i expected better tbh. we ended up having to rush bc service was so slow and almost missed the start of our show which was stressful. the manager walked by our table twice and didnt check in once

i genuinely think the food has potential like the ingredients seemed fresh but something is just off, maybe the kitchen is having an off night idk. but for what you pay and how u get treated i prob wont be back anytime soon. there are too many good spots in boston for this

ok so i literally had to make an account just to leave this review bc wow?? came here on a random tuesday w my boyfriend and we were not expecting much tbh like its a tiny little spot and the menu is kinda small but EVERYTHING we got was insane. the ramen broth tasted like it had been cooking for like a million years, so rich and deep and like actually warming from the inside out. we also got the gyoza and i am still thinking abt them and its been like 3 days lol. service was super chill and friendly, our server explained everything without being annoying abt it. prices are honestly so reasonable for nyc like we both ate and drank for under 60 bucks which feels impossible these days. already planning to go back next weekend ngl

ok so me and my boyfriend and his sister came here last saturday night and honestly i dont even know where to start lol. we waited like 45 mins after our reservation time which like fine whatever but nobody even came to tell us it would be that long?? we just stood there like idiots near the front. 

when we finally sat down the server took forever to come over and when she did she seemed super annoyed that we had questions about the menu. sorry i dont speak japanese lol?? 

the food was honestly just bad. i got the salmon teriyaki and it was so dry like cardboard i cant even describe it and my boyfriend got the ramen and said it tasted like instant noodles from a packet. his sister ordered sushi rolls and one of them was literally still kinda frozen in the middle which is nasty and also probably not safe?? 

the prices are NOT cheap either like we paid close to $120 for three people and left hungry and kinda grossed out. 

we asked to speak to a manager and someone came over and basically just shrugged at us. shrugged!! didnt offer to comp anything or even apologize properly. 

Nashville has so many good restaurants and this is NOT one of them. never coming back and telling everyone i know to skip it

ok so me and my boyfriend were literally SO excited to try this place bc we've been wanting to find a good kbbq spot in the city forever and our friend kept hyping it up so we finally made a reservation for a friday night

big mistake lol

first of all we waited like 40 mins PAST our reservation time and nobody even came to tell us what was going on?? we just stood there by the hostess stand like idiots while they seated walk ins ahead of us i'm pretty sure. when we finally sat down the grill at our table was like... not even working properly?? the smoke kept coming back in our faces the whole time and my hair smelled like meat for two days not in the fun way

the banchan was honestly so sad. like four tiny dishes and one of them was just plain cucumber. that's it. cucumber.

we ordered the galbi and it came out still pretty raw in the middle and when i asked our server about it she kinda just shrugged?? like ok thanks

the prices were NOT cheap either we spent like $85 for two people and left hungry and honestly kind of annoyed at each other lmao which is the worst part

idk maybe we caught them on a bad night but i really don't think i'm going back. there are way better options in chicago for real

came here w my sister and her bf last saturday and like... it was fine?? the lobster roll was actually pretty good ngl but my sister got the clam chowder and she said it was kinda bland. the vibe is nice tho, like its right by the water and we got a table outside which was cool. 

service was a lil slow tbh, we waited like 20 mins just to get our drinks order taken and the place wasnt even that packed. our waiter was nice tho once he actually came around lol

the prices r kinda steep for what you get, i paid like $28 for a plate of shrimp that was honestly just ok. nothing crazy. you can def find better seafood spots in the city for less $$

idk i wouldnt NOT go back but i also wouldnt be rushing to make a reservation ya know. good for like a casual night out if ur already in the area but probably wouldnt go outta my way for it. my sister said she'd come back for the lobster roll tho so maybe we'll give it another shot

ok so came here with my boyfriend and his sister last friday and like... it was fine?? the ramen was actually really good ngl, i got the tonkotsu and the broth was super rich and i slurped the whole thing lol. BUT we waited like 45 mins for a table which honestly wouldnt have been the worst thing except nobody told us it would be that long, we just kind of stood there awkwardly near the door

the gyoza were a little underwhelming tbh, tasted kinda frozen to me but maybe im wrong idk. my bf loved his bao so theres that

service was like... okay. our server was nice enough but forgot to bring waters for a while and we had to ask twice. small things but adds up u know

the vibe/decor is really cute tho ill give them that, lots of natural wood and these cool paper lanterns, very instagrammable if thats ur thing

prices are fair for portland i guess, we spent like 60ish with drinks which felt about right. i'd probably come back but wouldnt be my first choice on a busy weekend night, maybe a weekday lunch situation would be better. like 3 stars feels right, good food but the experience around it just needs some work

ok so like i really wanted to love this place bc everyone kept telling me to go here and i was so excited for a good pasta night but honestly?? super disappointed. the wait was like 45 mins on a tuesday which whatever i get it but then the food came out and my cacio e pepe was just... bland?? like where is the pepper, thats literally in the name lol. my bf got the chicken parm and he said it tasted like it came from a freezer bag which is not great for $24. the bread was good tho ill give them that. our server was nice enough but she disappeared for like 20 mins and we couldnt get more water which is always annoying. also the place is SO loud i couldnt even hear my bf talking across a small table. like if ur gonna charge these prices shouldnt the food at least be memorable?? idk maybe we just came on a bad night but i dont think ill be rushing back anytime soon. austin has way better italian spots for this price range tbh

so my buddies and i drove like 45 mins from aurora specifically for this place bc my coworker kept hyping it up saying it was "life changing bbq" lol ok dan... anyway

got there around 6 on a saturday and the wait wasnt bad maybe 20 mins which fine whatever. the vibe inside is cool tbh, lots of wood and neon signs and it smells amazing when you walk in which honestly got my hopes way up

heres the thing tho. the brisket was genuinely really good like i have no complaints there, super tender and the smoke ring was pretty on point. BUT the ribs were just... not it?? kinda dry and the sauce they use is way too sweet for my taste, felt like i was eating candy more than bbq

the sides were honestly forgettable. the mac n cheese tasted like the kraft box stuff and the cornbread was dry. for the prices they charge i expected a little more yknow

service was fine, our server was friendly but kinda disappeared for long stretches and we had to flag someone down for refills twice

look its not BAD bbq, its just not the transcendent experience dan promised me lmao. would i go back? maybe if i was already in the area but i def wouldnt make the drive again specifically for it. solid but not special

so my boyfriend has been OBSESSED with this place for like months and kept telling me we had to go, so finally last friday we made a reservation and went. and honestly?? it was just... fine lol

the hummus was really good ngl, like genuinely really good and the pita was warm which i appreciate bc some places just give u cold sad pita. but we ordered the lamb chops and they came out kinda lukewarm?? like not cold but def not hot and for $34 i feel like that shouldnt happen

the vibe is cute tho, very cozy and the candles everywhere made it feel romantic which was nice for date night. our server was sweet but she disappeared for like 20 mins at one point and we were sitting there with empty water glasses which is a pet peeve of mine lol

the falafel app was also solid, no complaints there. but my bf got the grilled fish and said it was kinda bland which was disappointing bc thats his whole thing w mediterranean food

like dont get me wrong i didnt hate it, i would prob go back if someone suggested it but i wouldnt like rush to make a rezzy again u know? feels like it has potential but maybe just had an off night. hoping next time is better bc the menu looks amazing on paper

ok so me and my friends came here last saturday and omg i literally cannot stop thinking about the ramen?? like we waited maybe 20 mins outside which honestly wasnt bad at all and the hostess was super nice about it

so theres like 4 of us and we all got different things so we could try everything lol. i got the tonkotsu ramen and my friend got the spicy miso and we kept stealing bites from each other the whole time lol. the broth was SO rich like how do they even do that. my other friend who doesnt even really like japanese food got the chicken katsu and she was literally obsessed by the end

the sake was really good too we got a carafe?? or whatever its called to share. prices were pretty reasonable tbh for boston like i was expecting to spend way more

only tiny thing is it was kinda loud inside so we had trouble hearing each other sometimes but honestly thats whatever its a fun vibe

our server was great too, really patient with us when we were being indecisive lmao. we prob asked her like 5 questions before ordering

def coming back and bringing more ppl, already texted my cousin about it who lives in cambridge. if ur even thinking about it just go

ok so me and my boyfriend were literally just looking for somewhere to grab tacos after the cubs game, nothing fancy just like decent street tacos you know?? walked past this place and it looked cute so we figured why not

BIG mistake lol

first off we waited like 20 mins to even get acknowledged and the place was maybe half full?? the hostess just kept walking past us like we were invisible, finally had to literally flag someone down. ordered the carne asada tacos and some queso and honestly i dont even know where to start. the queso tasted like straight up velveeta from a jar, like the kind you microwave for a super bowl party?? and the tacos.... my god. the meat was so dry and just tasted like nothing, no seasoning whatsoever. i put like four packets of hot sauce on it just to taste SOMETHING

and the prices!! $18 for tacos that tasted like that is actually criminal i'm sorry. my bf got the enchiladas and didnt even finish them and he literally eats everything

asked for more water twice and never got it

the chips were fine i guess but thats genuinely the only nice thing i can say

there is SO much good mexican food in chicago pls do yourself a favor and go literally anywhere else. lesson learned i guess 😭

ok so came here last saturday with my roommates bc we were all craving sushi and this place kept popping up on our feeds so we figured why not

honestly its fine?? like not bad but not like omg amazing either. the vibe inside is really cute tho ill give them that, lots of good lighting and the music wasnt too loud which was nice for actually being able to talk

we got the spicy tuna roll, some kind of baked salmon thing and the edamame obviously lol. the rolls were good but not like blew my mind good?? my roomate said hers tasted a little bland and i kinda agreed. the baked salmon roll was prob the best thing we ordered tbh

service was a bit slow like we waited probably 20 min for our food which ok its a friday but still. our server was nice tho when she came around

prices are pretty mid for LA sushi, not the cheapest but not insane either. we spent like $25 each which included drinks

idk i feel like theres better spots in the area but if ur nearby and hungry its def not gonna disappoint u either. would maybe come back to try the ramen bc i saw someones bowl walk by and it looked SO good. just not rushing back u kno

ok so i really wanted to like this place bc everyone keeps talking about it but honestly?? super disappointed. waited like 45 mins for a table which fine whatever but then the burger i got was just... mid. like the patty was kinda grey in the middle and the bun was soggy which is never a good sign lol. my friend got the mac and cheese and she said it tasted like the box stuff which like at that price point absolutely not

the fries were ok i guess but they were lukewarm by the time they got to our table and we were sitting right by the kitchen so idk what happened there

service was fine the girl was nice but she disappeared for a long time and we couldnt get refills forever. also the place is SO loud like i couldnt even hear my friend across the table

tbh if ur in portland just go to Luce instead, way better food for basically the same price and the vibe is actually nice and not giving me a headache. this place feels like it coasted on hype for too long and forgot to actually make the food good?? idk maybe ill give it another shot but probably not anytime soon

2 stars bc the server was sweet and the cocktail was decent but thats genuinely it

ok so honestly this place is pretty solid?? like i've driven past it a million times and finally stopped in last saturday and i'm kinda mad i waited so long lol. the burger was LEGIT like actually one of the better ones i've had in seattle tbh the patty was cooked perfect and the fries were crispy which like never happens. only reason i'm not giving 5 stars is the wait was kinda ridiculous for a tuesday?? took like 25 min just to get our drinks and the place wasn't even that packed. our server was nice tho no complaints there she was super apologetic about it. the mac and cheese side dish was also lowkey amazing didn't expect that. will def come back just maybe not on a weekend cause i can already tell that would be chaos

ok so honestly this place is like fine?? came here with my bf last saturday and the vibes were cute, dim lighting and everything but we waited like 45 mins for a table even tho we had a reservation which was kinda annoying ngl

the ramen was actually pretty good, i got the tonkotsu and the broth was REALLY rich which i loved but the noodles were a little mushy idk maybe they just sat too long. my bf got the spicy miso and he said it was mid lol. we also split some gyoza which were honestly the best part, crispy on the bottom and the filling was SO good

service was a little all over the place like our server was super nice but we had to ask twice for water refills and the food took forever to come out. not like unbearable but noticeable ya know

the prices are also kinda steep for what you get?? like $18 for a bowl of ramen is a lot when its not even blowing my mind. i feel like chicago has SO many good ramen spots and this one is just like...decent

i might come back to try their sushi menu bc a few ppl at other tables had these rolls that looked amazing but based on this visit alone its a solid 3 stars. not bad, not great, just kinda there

ok so i literally dont even know where to START with this place. went here last saturday with my boyfriend and his parents which was already stressful enough lol and everything just went completely wrong from the jump. waited like 45 mins for our table even tho we had a reservation?? the hostess just kept saying "a few more minutes" like girl its been almost an hour

and then the food omg. i got the lobster roll which was $38 BTW and it tasted like they just dumped a whole bottle of mayo on some rubbery pieces of who knows what and called it a day. my boyfriends dad got the clam chowder and he said it was cold in the middle like they just microwaved it halfway and gave up. honestly embarrassing for that price point

the waiter disappeared for like 20 mins at a time and when we asked for extra bread he just nodded and never came back lol classic

if u want actual good seafood in the city just go to Grand Banks over on the westside, totally different experience, fresh fish, nice staff, worth every penny. THIS place is just coasting on its location and charging tourist prices for absolute garbage quality

never coming back, told everyone i know to avoid it, 1 star is genuinely too generous

ok so i really wanted to like this place bc everyone kept hyping it up on instagram and tbh the vibe when you walk in is actually cute?? but like thats where the good stuff ends lol

the banchan was so sad. like 3 little dishes and one of them was just plain kimchi that tasted kinda old?? if you've ever been to Honey Butter in aurora you know what a real spread looks like, they give you like 6-7 sides and everything actually tastes homemade. this felt like an afterthought

we got the bulgogi and the spicy pork and honestly the portions were kinda small for the price, $18 for bulgogi that was like... fine. just fine. nothing memorable. my friend said it tasted a little sweet in a weird way not in a good caramelized way just like.. off

service was ok i guess our server checked on us maybe twice the whole meal and it was not even that busy on a tuesday night so idk what was going on there

the tofu soup was actually decent ill give them that, that was probably the highlight

idk maybe i went on a bad night but im not rushing back when there are better options around. might try again eventually but not anytime soon

ok so i've been meaning to write this for like 3 weeks but finally doing it lol. came here on a friday night with my boyfriend and his sister and honestly?? pretty solid experience overall. the banchan came out super fast and there were like 6 little dishes which is more than we got at Seoul Korean BBQ over on colfax (love that place but they only give you like 3-4 sides). 

the bulgogi was really tender and the portions were actually generous which like... doesn't always happen at these spots ya know. we also got the soon dubu jjigae and it was spicy in a good way, warmed me up bc it was freezing outside lol. 

only reason im not giving 5 stars is the service was a liiittle slow like it took forever to get someone to come light our grill and we had to ask twice for water refills. not a huge deal but just noticeable when the place wasn't even that packed tbh

the prices are pretty reasonable for denver like nothing on the menu felt crazy expensive which is refreshing. parking situation is kinda annoying tho, had to circle around for a bit

but yeah overall def coming back, the food more than makes up for the minor service stuff. would recommend if ur looking for kbbq in the area!!

ok so i come here like every other week now and i literally cannot stop thinking about their birria tacos?? the consomé alone is worth the drive honestly. service is always fast and the portions are HUGE for the price. my friends think im obsessed and yeah tbh i am. just go already you wont regret it

ok so ive been here twice now and im still kinda on the fence tbh. the bibimbap was actually really good like the stone pot was sizzling and everything came out perfect, but then my friend got the bulgogi and it was just... fine?? like nothing special. 

our waiter (i think his name was Jason or maybe Justin idk he had a blue shirt lol) was super nice and helped us figure out what to order since it was my first time. appreciated that a lot honestly. but we waited like 25 min for our food on a tuesday night which wasnt even that busy so idk what was going on there

the banchan sides were decent, the kimchi was good but they only gave us like 3 little dishes which felt kinda stingy compared to other korean places ive been to. 

parking is also kind of a nightmare just fyi if ur driving

i'll probably go back bc the bibimbap alone is worth it and the prices are pretty reasonable for nashville (everything here is getting so expensive omg) but i dont think its like blowing my mind or anything. solid spot if ur in the area but wouldnt go super out of my way for it

ok so like honestly this place is kind of a mixed bag?? the burger was actually really good ngl, like the patty was juicy and the brioche bun was toasted perfectly so props for that. BUT the fries were soggy and cold like they had been sitting there for 20 min before they brought them out. also our server was nice enough but disappeared for literally forever when we needed refills and the check. took us like 45 min to get out of there on a tuesday night which is just????? and for seattle prices i kinda expected a lil more ya know. the vibe is cute tho and parking wasnt a nightmare which is saying something around here lol. might come back and try the chicken sandwich but idk probably wont rush back anytime soon tbh

came here with my roommates last saturday night and it was just like... fine?? the burgers were decent nothing crazy but the fries were actually really good ngl. our server was nice enough but we waited foreverrr to get our food like almost 45 mins and the place wasnt even that packed so idk what was going on in the kitchen lol. 

the vibe is cute tho, very austin-y with all the exposed brick and stuff. drinks were a lil overpriced for what you get tbh, my margarita was basically just ice. one of my roommates got the chicken sandwich and said it was dry which tracks bc mine wasnt super juicy either. 

honestly its not bad its just not like... worth going out of your way for?? theres so many other spots in austin that do this same thing but better. we probably wont rush back but if someone suggested it id go again i guess. parking was also kind of a nightmare but thats just austin for u. 3 stars feels right, its solidly okay which is maybe worse than being bad bc at least then youd have a story lol

ok so honestly this place is like... fine?? came here with my boyfriend last saturday and the hummus was actually really good ngl, like super fresh and they gave you a ton of pita with it. but then our entrees took FOREVER to come out, we waited like 45 mins and the place wasnt even that packed?? 

the lamb kebabs were decent but mine was kinda dry tbh. my boyfriend got the shawarma wrap and said it was good but nothing he couldnt get somewhere else for cheaper. like is it that hard to just season the meat properly?? 

service was fine, our server was nice enough just seemed stretched thin or whatever. the vibe is cute inside tho ill give them that, nice lighting and they had some good music playing. 

drinks were a lil overpriced for what you get, paid like $14 for a cocktail that tasted mostly like juice lol. 

idk i might come back just for the hummus and apps honestly bc those were the highlight, but for a full dinner situation there are better spots in the city for similar price range. not bad just not great u know

ok so I've been meaning to write this for like forever but here we go lol. came here on a friday night with my bf and his sister and honestly?? it was pretty great. the pad see ew was SO GOOD like I'm still thinking about it and that was like 3 weeks ago. we also got the spring rolls as an app and those were solid, nothing crazy but still good.

only reason im not giving 5 stars is bc the wait was kinda long?? they said 20 mins and it was more like 40 which like ok I get it, it was busy, but still. and our server seemed a little frazzled tbh, forgot to bring extra napkins when we asked but she was nice so whatever

the curry my bf got was REALLY spicy even tho he ordered medium which lol he was sweating the whole time but he said it tasted amazing so take that how u will. portion sizes r generous which I always appreciate bc some thai places give u like 4 bites and charge u $18 you know??

prices are pretty reasonable for chicago, we spent like $65 for 3 ppl with drinks which is not bad at all. the vibe inside is cute, kinda dim lighting, good for a date night situation. will def be back to try the drunken noodles next time!!

so my boyfriend and I have been trying to find a good mediterranean spot ever since we moved to portland like 8 months ago and honestly this place kept popping up so we finally tried it last saturday

ok so the hummus was REALLY good like genuinely one of the better ones ive had, super smooth and the olive oil on top was chefs kiss. we also got the falafel plate and it was fine?? like not bad but not omg amazing either they were a little dry tbh

but heres the thing the service was kinda all over the place, our server was sweet but forgot my drink twice and we waited foreverrr for our entrees like almost 40 mins on a not even that busy night. my lamb kabob came out lukewarm which was disappointing bc i could tell the seasoning was actually really good it just needed to be hotter ya know

the vibe is cute tho, nice lighting, felt cozy. and prices are pretty reasonable for portland which like,, that's saying something lol

idk i feel like this place has the potential to be really solid but something just felt a lil off. maybe we caught them on an off night?? we might try it again but probably not rushing back. solid 3 stars, hope they work out the kinks

ok so came here with my roommates last friday night and like... it was fine?? the bulgogi was actually pretty good ngl but we waited like 45 min for a table which nobody told us about when we walked in. would've been nice to know lol

the banchan was decent, got like 5 or 6 little dishes which i appreciated but some of them tasted kinda bland tbh. my roommate got the sundubu jjigae and she said it was good but not as spicy as she wanted even after she asked them to make it spicier

service was a lil all over the place like our server was sweet but kept forgetting stuff, had to ask for water refills twice and we never got the extra rice we ordered until we were basically done eating lmao

the vibe is nice tho!! super cozy inside and the kpop playing in the background was a fun touch. parking was kind of a nightmare but thats seattle so whatever

honestly i might come back and give it another shot bc i feel like it has potential, just felt like an off night maybe?? or maybe we hit it at a weird time. not bad not amazin, right in the middle for me

ok so we came here last saturday w my sister and her bf and omg i was NOT expecting it to be this good?? like we just picked it randomly bc the place we wanted to go had a 2 hr wait lol. best accidental decision ever tbh

the ramen was insane. i got the tonkotsu and the broth was so rich like i literally drank the whole thing which i never do. my sister got some kind of spicy miso situation and she kept making these little noises the whole time 😂 we also split the gyoza and an order of karaage and honestly the karaage might be the best ive had outside of actual japan (went in 2019 so im basically an expert lmao)

service was super chill and friendly, our server came and checked on us like the right amount of times u know? not too much not ignoring us. the place was pretty packed for a tuesday night which says a lot i think

only tiny thing is parking was a lil annoying but thats like every place in seattle so whatever not taking off a star for that

we are def coming back, already texted the group chat. my bro in law who is super picky about japanese food needs to try this place asap. 10/10 no notes

ok so i literally was NOT expecting nashville to have seafood this good?? like i moved here from the coast and honestly gave up on finding decent fish in this city but omg i was so wrong. went here last saturday with my boyfriend and we got the blackened redfish and the lobster mac and i am still thinking about that lobster mac lol it was so creamy and rich we almost ordered a second one tbh. our waiter Marcus was SO nice and funny, checked on us like the right amount of times ya know not too much not too little. also he remembered i asked about the gluten free options and came back to double check with the kitchen without me even having to ask again which was really sweet. the vibe is kinda chill but also feels a little fancy which i loved for date night. drinks were strong which i always appreciate lol. only tiny thing is the wait was like 45 min on a saturday but they texted us when the table was ready so we just walked around the area which was fine. honestly just go, dont overthink it, get the lobster mac and thank me later

ok so i've been meaning to write this for weeks lol but finally doing it - this place is SO good like genuinely one of the better indian spots in austin rn. we got the butter chicken and some saag paneer and both were honestly really solid, the butter chicken especially had like this really nice depth to it?? not just sweet like some places make it

the naan was fresh and you could tell, not that sad rubbery stuff

only reason im not giving 5 stars is the service was a lil slow and we had to ask twice for water which like... cmon. also parking situation is kinda annoying but thats austin for you tbh

compared to taj palace over on burnet i think the food here is actually better but taj has better service so its kind of a tradeoff depending on what ur in the mood for. price wise they're pretty similar

we'll def be back tho, my bf is already talking about ordering the lamb rogan josh next time which we didnt try this visit. the space itself is cute too, nice vibe for a date night or whatever. just maybe go when ur not super hungry bc the wait can stretch a bit

overall really good spot, austin needed more places like this fr

ok so i really wanted to like this place bc its like 2 blocks from my apt and i've been looking for a good indian spot in the area forEVER but nope. ordered the butter chicken and it tasted like... tomato soup?? like the kind from a can?? zero depth to it, barely any spice, and the naan was so thick and doughy it was basically raw in the middle. sent it back and the waiter (tall guy with glasses, think his name was Derek or something idk) was SO condescending about it, like sorry but im not gonna eat raw bread my dude. 

also waited like 45 mins for food on a tuesday night when the place was maybe half full. not busy at all. no explanation, nobody came to check on us. my friend got the saag paneer and said it was fine but "fine" is not what im paying $18 for. the mango lassi was honestly the only thing that slapped but i can make that at home lol

idk maybe they're having an off night but based on this i'm not coming back. bummed bc the menu looked so good online 😭

ok so like i really wanted to love this place bc everyone keeps hyping it up but honestly?? pretty disappointing visit. we got the pad see ew and the panang curry and the curry was just... bland?? like where is the flavor lol. it tasted like they forgot to season it or something idk. the pad see ew noodles were weirdly mushy too not the texture ur supposed to have at all

our waiter (tall guy, i think his name was marcus or something?) was nice enough but we waited foreverrr to even get our waters and the place wasnt even that busy. like maybe 4 other tables?? no excuse for that

the spring rolls were actually decent ill give them that, crispy and the dipping sauce was good. but two decent spring rolls dont make up for a mediocre entree situation

also $18 for the curry felt steep for what we got. denver has so many good thai spots and this just doesnt compete imo. might have just been an off night but idk if im willing to risk another $50+ meal to find out. probably wont be back unless someone drags me here

ok so i've been here like 3 times now and honestly its pretty solid?? the butter chicken is SO good, like genuinely one of the better ones i've had in denver. service was a lil slow last time but nbd. portions are decent for the price. will def be back, just wish the naan was a bit thicker lol

ok so i dont even know where to start lol. came here last saturday with my boyfriend bc we kept seeing it pop up on instagram and honestly we shouldve just stayed home and made hotdogs or something

waited like 45 mins for a table which fine whatever its popular i get it. but then our waiter (i think his name was travis?? tall guy with the beard) literally disappeared for like 20 minutes after taking our drink order. we had to flag down someone else just to get water

ordered the brisket platter which everyone online is RAVING about and it came out lukewarm?? like not even close to hot. the meat was so dry i needed three cups of water to choke it down and the sauce they give you on the side doesnt even help bc its weirdly sweet in a bad way. my bf got the pulled pork sandwich and he took two bites and just kinda put it down which says everything

the cornbread was honestly the best part and thats not saying much bc it was from a box i guarantee it

paid $34 for that platter btw. THIRTY FOUR DOLLARS for dry lukewarm meat and a disappearing act from travis. portland has actual good bbq spots please do yourself a favor and find one of them instead

ok so my cousin was visiting from texas and she kept saying she missed good bbq and i was like omg i know this place lets go, huge mistake lol. we waited like 45 mins which fine whatever its bbq i get it but then the brisket came out and it was just... grey?? like not even a little pink, totally dried out and the "smoke ring" was nonexistent. my cousin literally laughed and was like "this is what yall consider bbq up here" and honestly i wanted to disappear 

the sauce was way too sweet like ketchup with sugar in it basically and there was barely any bark on the ribs at all. also our server disappeared for a solid 20 mins after dropping the food off and we needed napkins desperately bc bbq lol. had to flag down someone else entirely

and for the prices?? we paid almost $80 for two people and left hungry and kind of sad about it. my cousin ended up making us brisket at my apartment the next day on my little indoor grill and it was genuinely better and she was working with like a $15 piece of meat from safeway

seattle i love you but we gotta do better. this place is coasting on vibes and exposed brick. not coming back

ok so i literally was NOT expecting to love this place as much as i did?? my friend dragged me here after i suggested like three other places and honestly i owe her an apology lol. walked in and the vibe is just so cozy, like warm lighting and that smell of like garlic and herbs hits you immediately and ur just like ok yeah we're staying

the hummus. THE HUMMUS. i dont even know what they put in it but it tastes nothing like the stuff from whole foods i've been eating thinking i knew what hummus was. served warm with this olive oil drizzled on top and some pita that was clearly freshly made like ?? come on. 

got the lamb kebabs as my main and they were so tender i honestly had a moment. my friend got the falafel plate and kept making sounds while eating which is always a good sign lmao

service was super chill, our guy checked on us without being annoying about it which is all i ask honestly. prices are really reasonable for austin too which like.... rare these days ngl

we split the baklava at the end and i'm still thinking about it three days later. already planning to come back next weekend and i'm bringing my whole family. do NOT sleep on this place if ur in austin

ok so I've been meaning to write this for like 3 weeks lol but better late than never!! came here on a tuesday night with my bf and his sister and honestly we were not disappointed. the falafel wrap was SO GOOD like crispy on the outside but not dry?? that's hard to pull off tbh. we also got the hummus plate to share and omg the pita was warm and fresh it literally melted in ur mouth

service was pretty good, our server was super friendly and checked on us a few times without being annoying about it which I appreciate. it did take kinda long to get our food but it wasn't like crazy busy so that was a lil weird, hence the 4 stars not 5

the vibe inside is really cozy, lots of little lights and it smells amazing when u walk in. portions are generous too which like YES thank u. my bf got the lamb kebab and he literally did not stop talking about it the whole drive home lmao

prices are reasonable for portland which honestly is saying something these days 😅 parking was a bit of a struggle but thats just portland for u. will def be back, already thinking about that wrap again ngl

ok so i really wanted to like this place bc the pics on here looked SO good and my friend kept hyping it up but like… idk man it was just kind of a disappointment overall??

we waited like 45 mins past our reservation time which like ok fine stuff happens but nobody came over to like acknowledge us or offer us a drink or anything we just stood there like idiots near the host stand lol

when we finally got seated the bread came out and it was honestly not bad thats probably the highlight tbh. but then my pasta (the cacio e pepe) came out and it was just… clumpy?? like the noodles were all stuck together and it tasted weirdly bland for something that should be peppery and rich. my boyfriend got the chicken parm and said it was "fine" which is NOT what you wanna hear when ur paying $28 for it

the server was nice enough i guess but she disappeared for like forever and we couldnt get a refill on water to save our lives

Chicago has SO many good italian spots and this just doesnt compete at that price point honestly. maybe ill give it another shot someday but like… probably not anytime soon. the vibes were cute tho so theres that i guess

ok so i literally cannot believe i paid $45 for a lobster roll that tasted like it came from a gas station?? the bread was soggy, the lobster smelled kinda off and our server disappeared for like 20 mins. never coming back honestly. nyc has SO many good seafood spots this is not one of them lol

omg I just had to drop my 2 cents on this lil' spot in Miami, it's called Siam Breeze, and like WOW, totally didn't expect to leave so happy, ya know? So, first things first, when I walked in the place was super chill, quite cozy and not pretentious AT ALL, like a good friend’s living room with THAI MAGIC happening in the back.

I went for the pad thai cuz like duh, that's a must-try, I think it had just the right amount of everything - spicy, sweet, tangy... yes, yes, and YES. Actual noodles danced on my taste buds, no joke!! That being said, I SPILLED some on my shirt, total clutz nods to me😂. Also got the green curry, which pretty much knocked my socks off, it felt like a hug in a bowl, piping hot and delish! Just to mention tho, if you’re not into spicy stuff, maybe have a drink ready, cuz it has a little punch.

The staff were super friendly, ever had a waiter that felt like your bestie after like 5 mins? Yeah, that’s the vibe here, loved it! Only bummer? It took a little time for the food to come out but I guess that’s the price for HOT food instead of reheated stuff, so I'm not mad just a gentle heads up if ur in a hurry.

Overall, Siam Breeze is deffo a place I'll hit

So me and a couple buddies decided to hit up this Mexican joint over the weekend, and honestly, it was kinda meh. I was super excited for some killer tacos, right? I mean, who doesn't love tacos, but idk, this place just didn’t hit the spot. For starters, the service was like totally slow like we had to flag down our waiter just to get some water and it wasn't even that busy. Like, what the heck?

When the food finally showed up, it was, uh, not what I was expecting. My enchiladas were kinda soggy and the sauce was just... blah? My friend's tacos were okayish, but nothing to write home about. Oh, and I feel like they drowned my beans in cheese for some reason. I'm all for cheese, don't get me wrong, but this was a cheese flood.

Also, the vibe was sorta off. The music was way loud like we were yelling over each other the whole time and nobody wants that during dinner, ya know? I will say, on the plus side, the salsa they gave us was pretty bomb, so like they got that right at least.

I mean, I've had better Mexican food tbh, and def in no rush to come back. Kinda bummed, but hey, maybe it was just an off night or something. 🤷‍♂️

So like, I was super excited to try this new Thai spot in Nashville cuz I'm obsessed with pad thai and like someone told me it's the best they've had, but tbh it just didn't hit the mark for me. I rolled in there with my friend, and the place was kinda cute, like nothing fancy but that's cool, I'm not fancy either lol.

Anyway, we ordered the pad thai and green curry. First of all, the pad thai was, idk, sorta bland? Like I didn’t get that punch of flavor, you know? Kinda tasted like it was missing something, maybe more lime or peanuts or just idk something! The green curry was, how do I put this... watery af? I was like, this isn’t soup, guys. I’ve def had better at this little spot in East Nashville, dang, what's it called, oh yeah Thai Esane, that place rocks.

Service was alright tho, the server was really nice and all, but yeah, the food didn’t really make it for me. Maybe it was an off night or something. Would give it another shot but I think I gotta stick with my go-to spots for now. 🤷‍♂️

So, me and a couple friends decided to hit up this Japanese place in Nashville cause like we'd heard some good stuff, but idk, it was kinda meh. We got there and the vibe seemed pretty cool, like the decor was on point, but service was super slow, like we legit waited forever just to get menus.

When food FINALLY came out, I gotta say I was kinda disappointed. I got the sushi platter, and tbh it wasn't all that fresh. One of my friends tried the ramen, and he said it tasted kinda bland, like it was missing something, maybe seasoning? Also, it took so long I low-key thought they forgot about us.

Our server was really sweet, but like just one person trying to handle way too many tables. Feel like they needed more staff or something. Price wasn’t bad, but like for that level of food, didn't really feel worth it, you know? Probs not coming back anytime soon, but like maybe they were having an off night or something? Who knows 🤷‍♀️.

So I finally tried Porky’s BBQ in LA and tbh I was not dissapointed! I'm usually a Woody’s BBQ fan (they've got that amazing brisket you just can't quit lol) but figured why not branch out right? 😅 

Walked in, kinda smelling like heaven, you know that smokey, tangy aroma that just lingers in the air, yessss pls. Went with the pulled pork sandwich and, dudes, it was juicy af. Like drip down your chin level, seriously. The sauce had a nice kick but wasn’t trying to burn your face off, if you know what I mean. 

The slaw was alright, not my fave, might be a bit meh compared to the one at Woody’s but totally passable. Sides wise, the mac n cheese HIT the spot, super cheesy (and probs not on anyone's diet plan lol). Oh oh and customer service was A+. Staff was chill, not in your face but super helpful when I couldn’t decide wth I wanted hahaha.

It was a bit crowded ngl, but it’s LA whatchu gonna do 🤷‍♂️ Prices def comparable to Woody’s, so that’s a win. Overall, a solid 4 stars. I'll def be back when my BBQ craving strikes, but Woody's still got that special place in my heart.

Okay, so I hit up this American place in Seattle last weekend bc I heard mixed things and was like, why not give it a shot right? The vibe was kinda cool, like they tried to do a modern-rustic kinda thing but is it still rustic if you can see dust hanging out on the decor? 🤔 Anyway, the menu had all the usual suspects, burgers, steaks, some salads which let's be real, no one comes here for salads unless ur on a diet and lowkey miserable.

I ordered this burger, and tbh, it was just okay? Like, the patty was a bit dry, not the juicy goodness I was expecting. Fries were hit or miss, some were crispy, some were limp - i'm not picky but they prob coulda done better. Service was actually p. friendly tho! Our waitress had a huge smile and kept our drinks topped. 

Dessert was a bit of a rollercoaster, I got this chocolate cake thing and idk was it really cake or just a dry brown sponge?? Not sure lol. But they had this caramel sauce that kinda made up for it, so it wasn't a total bust. 

I guess if you're in the area and wanna grab a bite without crazy expectations, it's fine. Not somewhere I'd run to again tho unless someone else is payin lol.

So like last night I hit up this Korean spot in LA, honestly can't remember the name but it's the one with all the neon lights outside. You can't miss it. Walking in, it was all cozy and smelled amazing like you could tell they know what's up with the BBQ. Started off with the kimchi, and man, it's like a party of flavors in your mouth! 

We got the galbi and bulgogi, and omg can you even call it dinner if there's no sizzling going on?? It's so fun doing the whole cook-it-yourself thing, like interactive dinner is the best dinner. 

Also, their soju selection is on point, like I wanted to try everything. Service was super friendly, they kept checking in on us to make sure we didn't burn anything (bless them lol). Dessert was this shaved ice, bingsoo or something, and it was HUGE, like a mountain of sweet icy goodness.

Def going back, maybe I'll even remember the name next time 😂. Who needs a fancy name anyway when the food's that good, right?

OMG y'all, if u haven't tried Zaytoon in LA, ur seriously missing out!!! Just left & I'm like still dreaming about the falafel platter—soo crispy and flavorful, legit the best I've had in forever. Their hummus is like, out of this WORLD creamy, idk what they put in it but I'm obsessed. 🌿🍴

The vibe is super chill, kinda like ur cool friend's backyard party, u feel me? Also, shoutout to the waiter, I think his name is Amir?? Sucha gem, made us feel at home & had great recs. We were thinking it'd be just another 'meh' dinner, but nahhhh.

Def try the spicy feta dip & baklava for dessert, ur tastebuds will thank u!! Went with my crew and everyone loved it, no complaints whatsoever. Perfect spot for date night or just chilling with friends. Can’t wait to go back! 🙌🥙

Yo, this place is fire!! Went for the burgers and like, omg they did not disappoint. 😍 The drinks were on point too, def a must-visit if ur in Nashville. Music vibes are awesome—real like that South charm. Staff super chill and friendly. 5 stars without a doubt. 🌟

Ugh where do I even start with this place?? Sooo disappointed, I was sooo looking forward to some good Indian food but wow just no. First off it took like FOREVER to get seated even tho the place was half empty?? What r they even doing??? Anyway, ordered butter chicken, my fav 😍 but when it finally came out it was like swimming in oil and had like NO flavor, lemme tell ya I had better from a jar 🤢. Asked for naan and some rice and they brought it out ICE cold, barely edible omg. And the staff just seemed like they couldn't care less, kept on ignoring us the WHOLE time like HELLO?? you're gettin paid for this!!?? Def not going back there ever again unless they learn how to cook and actually treat ppl like customers instead of invisible ghosts. Save your money peeps.

So I tried out this Italian joint in Boston the other night with a couple of friends and honestly, it was like a mixed bag for me. The place had this really chill vibe, and the staff were super FRIENDLY, gotta give 'em that. But like, the food was kinda all over the place.

I ordered the spaghetti carbonara because duh, classic! It was okaaaay but like, kinda underwhelming? The pasta was a bit overcooked for my taste, and I swear the sauce was a little bland. My friend had the lasagna, tho, and she was RAVING about it, said it was super cheesy and delish, so maybe I just picked the wrong dish? ¯\_(ツ)_/¯

The wine selection was pretty decent tbh, and we had a bottle of chianti that really hit the spot. Also, props for the garlic bread, it was BOMB. But ngl the prices were a bit high for what you're getting. I might give this place another shot cause it's close by and maybe try that lasagna next time. Not a total win, but worth a visit if you’re around!

OMG where do I even start with this place lol. First off, came here with like some friends last weekend 'cause we heard it was, y'know, "the place to be" for seafood in Miami. Yeah no. Total letdown, ugh. 

So we get there and like, the vibe is kinda off, like wait staff just seemed kinda lost n distracted? It took forever to even get our drinks (and we’re just talkin' waters), so that set the tone pretty well. 

When the food finally did arrive, I'm like, wth is this?? The shrimp was totally rubbery and the lobster tasted weirdly fishy, like isn't that the one thing it shouldn’t taste like? IDK, maybe it was a bad batch or something but no one at the table liked theirs either. 

And OMG, the prices?? Feels like we paid more than we got for sure, like it's seafood but still. At the end of the meal, we were just tryna get outta there fast—didn't even bother with dessert even though we usually do. Tbh, not planning to come back anytime soon. Miami's got plenty more places to try. 🙄

So like, the sushi was kinda meh? Friendly staff tho, and the vibe is chill. But tbh, I've had better spicy tuna rolls from a gas station. Kinda pricey for what it is 🙃, but hey it's LA lol. Might come back if I'm in the area but probs not goin outta my way.

So like, I finally checked out Il Cucina on 5th Ave and honestly, it's pretty dang good! 😋 I went there with my buddy and we were both starving, which is like, the best way to eat Italian food IMO. We started with the bruschetta and like, yum??? So fresh and garlicky! Then I had the chicken parm and my friend got the seafood pasta. Both were solid!! The chicken was crispy but not like overdoing it and the pasta was loaded with shrimp and scallops, like for real! 

I’d say it’s a bit pricier than that other spot I usually hit up in Little Italy, La Trattoria, but the vibe here was sorta cozier? And props for not being stingy with the parmesan on everything lol, love that. The service was a tad slow, but it was kinda packed so whatevs, not a deal breaker. Def coming back when I crave Italian, but might still hit La Trattoria when I'm feeling a bit cheap jk. Still, def recommend it if you’re in the area!! 🍝🍷

OMG, so I just hit up this place in Portland called Joe's Diner and WOW, I am like STILL thinking about the meal. If you’re into American comfort food this spot is gonna be your JAM. I can't even with their burgers – it's like they were made by burger-loving angels, seriously. 🤤

I had the bacon cheeseburger and lemme tell you, it's not just a meal, it's a whole experience. The bacon was crispy, the cheese all melty, and the patty was JUICY AF. The fries are like perfectly seasoned too, so good I almost ordered another round but I wanted to try dessert too. TBH the portions are pretty huge, so come hungry or be prepared to take some home!!

Also, the staff? SUPER friendly. Our server was so chill and made us feel like total VIPs. They also have this killer milkshake menu that’s like next level. I got the chocolate one and I’m not kidding, it was like drinking a sweet dream.

The vibe inside is real laid-back and cozy, like the kinda place you'd wanna hangout all afternoon. Anyway, gotta say, this was one of the best meals I’ve had in Portland. Like just go there already! You'll thank me later. 🍔🍟❤️

so me + my friends hit up this spot last weekend cuz we were craving mediterranean food but tbh it was kinda underwhelming. like, we walked in and the vibe was chill, but then the service was sooo slow it was like we were invisible or something. waited forever for someone to even take our order and we were all starving 😩

i got the falafel plate and like, it was okay, but kinda dry? idk maybe it was an off day but honestly i expected more. my friend got the lamb kabob and she said it was decent but nothing to write home about, ya know? also, portions were small for the price, we basically left still kinda hungry.

the hummus was pretty good tho, gotta give them that. so like, that’s why they’re getting 2 stars instead of 1. plus, it’s NY, there's SO many options out there, i prob won't be coming back here anytime soon. just wanna keep it real ✌️

Okay so like, I went to this spot last weekend for dinner 'cuz my friend was hyping it up and honestly? I was kinda disappointed. I mean, don't get me wrong, the place looks cute and all but when it came down to the food... not so great. It's like, how do you mess up a burger in Chicago, right? Mine was def overcooked and kinda dry, and the fries were like meh, super basic and kinda cold tbh. Our server was nice tho, so props for that, but it took forever to get our food, and the place wasn't even that busy? Like, make it make sense. I guess it's a vibe if you just wanna grab drinks, but if you're heading there to eat, maybe keep your expectations low, you know? Not rushing back anytime soon.

so i was rlly craving some Thai food n decided to hit up this spot in Seattle, and idk man, it was kinda disappointing tbh. like i walked in with high hopes cuz i heard some good stuff but it was a letdown. first, lemme say the vibe was okay, but kinda cramped n noisy, not in a fun way, more like can’t-even-hear-myself-think kinda way. service was meh? our server was nice but MIA half the time 🤷‍♂️

I got the Pad Thai, which is usually my go-to, ya know? and idk how, but it was legit bland. it’s like they forgot to put flavor in it (is that even possible?) it didn't have that punch you expect and crave from thai food. my buddy ordered the green curry n it was just sooo heavy, like soup with a coconut milk overdose. 🙄

honestly, i’ve had way better at that other place over on 45th (if u know u know), they're always on point with the flavors and the portions aren't skimpy either. guess this is a reminder to stick to my usual spots 🤷‍♀️ probs wont be back unless im actually out of options.

So me and my buddies decided to try this BBQ joint cuz, ya know, we’re always on the hunt for good brisket in Austin (duh). Anyway, we roll up hungry and all, and man, what a letdown 🥲. First off, the line was like ridic long and I’m thinking, “cool, this place must be good if everyone’s waiting?” But nah. Just a front.

We finally get to the counter and the dude there seemed pretty checked out, like not rude or nothing but just like, not really interested? We order our stuff and when it comes out, I'm like, this is it? The brisket was dry af, like sure, slather on some sauce and maybe it'll be okay but I kinda feel like you shouldnt have to save meat w sauce, ya know? My buddy got the ribs and they were just crusty and not in the good way, more like chewin on a shoe kinda way.

Also, they forgot my side of mac n cheese, I had to remind them twice. By the time I got it, everyone was pretty much done eating and I had like 5 bites left of my sad dry plate. And here’s the kicker, we all paid wayyy more than what it was worth. Like why is bad food so overpriced ugh.

I guess props for the atmosphere being alright with cool vibes or whatever, but honestly, it doesn't make up for the lackluster bbq. We

Okay so I hit up this BBQ place in Chi-Town coz a friend was raving about it, and honestly, I went in with like super high hopes. The vibes? Totally chill, loved the decor, and the service was alright. But the food... meh. I got the ribs, which were like okayish but could've deffo been juicier. I'm a sauce person and they kinda skimped on that. The sides tho were like, legit too salty?? I tried to wash it down with a couple beers b/c they have a decent selection, but man, at the end I felt like I needed to chug a gallon of water LOL. Also they forgot my friend’s order so we waited forever and it’s like, c'mon guys. I’d say it’s a decent spot if ur in the area but maybe my expectations were way too high, idk.

Oh man, so lemme just say, I was pretty pumped to try this place 'cause my friend kept hyping it up, like she wouldn’t shut up about their sushi. So I roll in thinking it'll be mind-blowing or whatever, but ughh, super meh. First off, it took forever to get seated even tho it wasn't even that busy?? IDK what was up with that. The vibe was alright but like kinda generic. 

The sushi was okayish but didn’t really make me wanna dance with happiness, you know? The fish was not super fresh and tasted kinda bland, needed way more flavor. Plus, like, the service was kinda slow and our waiter seemed distracted or maybe just had a bad day?? idk. For the prices they charge, I expected way more. Coulda used an extra wasabi kick or something LOL. Honestly, not worth the detour or hype, maybe just go somewheres else. 🤷‍♂️

OMG lemme just say I was SO hyped for this place, like my friend Jake won't shut up about it so I thought okay, let's give it a go. Big mistake. We were heading back from a hike and starving, and all I wanted was some good ribs, you know? But like, I walk in and it's like walking into a time warp or something - dingy 90s vibe and the weirdest cowboy decor ever.

Okay and so we get seated and wait FOREVER for a server to even show up, like are they even working? And when she finally does, she acts like I'm really inconveniencing her or something. Not cool, lady.

Food finally arrives and OMG I'm sorry but like, how do you mess up BBQ this bad? Ribs were dry af, like chewing on wood. Sauce was weirdly tangy but not in a good way, more like i think it came from a bottle kind of way. And the sides??? Mac n cheese was like from a box, salad barely had any dressing, not even worth mentioning.

Not coming back ever again. Srsly, if ur looking for good BBQ, go literally anywhere else in Denver. Save urself the trouble and I guess I'll be side-eying Jake's food recs from now on lol.

Okay, so first off, lemme just say I didn't even plan on coming here. My friends and I were like wandering around Boston and stumbled into this spot cuz we were all honestly just starving lol.

So, it’s like one of those places that looks super cute inside, nice vibes and all, but then the service was kinda meh. Our server seemed really busy or somethin' cuz we waited forever just to get drinks. When the food finally came out, it was decent! I ordered the burger and it was okay, like not mind-blowing but def not bad. My friend got a salad, which felt kinda basic for the price we paid tbh.

However, I gotta say, the fries were super crispy and good, so that was a win for me! Drinks were okay too, but a bit pricey, like c'mon Boston, chill a little with the prices. 

Overall, the place was average. Not sure if I'd like go out of my way to come back, but it didn't totally suck either. 3 stars cuz there’s definitely room for improvement.

So my friends and I hit up this Italian spot in Miami becuz ya know, we were craving some pasta and stuff. Tbh, had high hopes, the vibe was like super nice and chill. We sat outside, bc Miami weather is like the best for that rn. Anyways, the food was kinda a mixed bag. 

I ordered the carbonara, and it was okay ish? Like, the pasta was cooked legit perfect, but the sauce was not as creamy as I wanted, kinda bland tbh. My friend tried the margherita pizza, and like in all honesty, it was good but not OMG amazing like I expected from an italian place y'know? Another friend got the lasagna and said it was decent but nothing to write home about.

Service was like pretty solid tho, our waitress was super friendly and kept our drinks topped up which I appreciated. Also, dessert was bomb, we shared tiramisu, and it kinda saved the night. Pricey for what it is, tbh probs not rushing back anytime soon but I'd say it's worth a shot for dessert at least. 🤷‍♀️

So like I tried this Thai spot the other night, and ngl, I wasn't super impressed. 😕 First off, the service was kinda slow, like I get it, everyone's busy, but I sat there wondering if I'd, like, suddenly become a ghost or something. When the food did show up, it was meh. The pad thai was pretty bland, def needed more of that kick that makes Thai food awesome. 🙃 And idk if it was just me but the chicken in my curry was super chewy, honestly felt like I was gnawing on rubber. The only redeeming thing was maybe the Thai iced tea, but is it even possible to mess that up? Anyway, probs not going back anytime soon. Too many other good places in LA to settle for this, ya know? 🌟🌟

ugh tbh kinda disappointed, esp w the hype on this place. tried the falafel wrap n it was meh, like way soggy and not enough sauce or smth? service was super slow too even tho it wasnt that busy. hummus was decent but nothin special. maybe just an off day?? idk, prolly won't rush back 🙄

So like, I checked out this Korean place in Chicago the other night, and honestly, it was kinda meh. The place was called uh, Kimchi House or something, don't quote me on that, lol. I was really hyped for some bibimbap cus it's my fave, but man was I disappointed.

The bibimbap was like, missing something—I dunno, maybe it needed more flavor or like, seasoning? It was kinda bland which is so not what I'm used to, like c'mon spice stuff up, y'know? Plus, they only gave me this tiny bowl of sauce on the side, like they're rationing it or smth. 

Also, our waiter, I think his name was Kevin or Keith?? Was not very helpful tbh. We asked for more napkins he sorta took his sweet time, kinda felt like he wasn't really vibing there, which I get, we've all had those days, but still. 

Gonna say 2 stars cuz the ambiance was chill and the banchan (side dishes) were alright, but idk, the main stuff needs some work if I'm gonna go back.

Omg this place is like a lil pocket of Mediterranean sunshine right in Boston. Seriously, if you haven't eaten at Zaytuna, what even are you doing with your life? Went there w/ some friends and I'm still dreaming about the falafel. It’s legit fluffy on the inside and crispy outside, ugh so good. My friend got the lamb kebab and I was kinda jealous but like nbd cuz we shared (pro tip: go w/ ppl who share lol). The hummus is smooth af, and idk what magic they sprinkle on their pita but dang, it's the best I've had. Oh and the baklava? Sweet crispy heaven 😍. The staff were super chill and cool too, which is always a plus. Already planning my next visit, who’s in? 🍴🔥

so I was really hyped about checking out this Japanese spot in Nashville, like my friend said it was supposed to be the best. But man, idk what happened. Started with the sushi, and I gotta say it felt like the fish jumped on a plane from Japan and got stuck in customs bc it wasn’t fresh at all. I've had way better at Sakura down the street, and that’s like not even their specialty, yknow?

The ramen broth was kinda disappointing too, like I was expecting that rich flavor but it was more like flavored water? not tryna be mean but it just didn't do it for me. The noodles were okay I guess but nothing to write home about.

Service was just meh. Our server was friendly but they were running around like crazy, seemed kinda overwhelmed or somethin, so it was hard to get a refill. Also, the wait was like forever even tho it wasn’t that busy idk why.

Decor was cool tho, they’ve got these rad lights and stuff but honestly, I’d rather just eat at Sakura where it’s less about the looks and more about the flavors, if you know what I mean. Sorry but prolly not coming back to this one. 😕

sooo went to this Indian spot in Boston last nite. food was ok, like not bad but not mind-blowing either?? naan was kinda chewy, and the curry was a bit too salty for me. drinks were strong tho, so that's a plus 😂 service was friendly if a bit slow... honestly might try again but in no hurry.

OMG, you guys!! 😍 If ur in NYC and haven't been to Kimchi Kingdom yet, ur SERIOUSLY missing out!! I went there last night with some friends, and like, wow. Just WOW. We crushed SO MUCH food and honestly, my taste buds are still doing a happy dance.

We started with the kimchi pancakes and lemme tell you, they were crispy af and just the right amount of spicy. I could eat those ALL day. Then moved on to the bulgogi beef and it was so tender it practically melted in my mouth, not even kidding. And don’t get me started on the bibimbap—it's like comfort food but more exciting. 🔥

Also, the staff there is super nice and like, really chill? Our waiter legit felt like a friend by the end of it. AND the vibe inside is also super cozy, perfect for catching up with friends or just stuffing your face with delish food.

If you’re not stuffing your gob with kimchi fried rice from here at least once, ur doing NYC wrong. Cannot WAIT to go back!!! 💯

So like I wanted to treat myself after work and hit up this Japanese spot and tbh I'm kinda disappointed. 🙄 I mean the place LOOKS nice, like dim lights and all cozy vibes, BUT the food...man, it missed the mark big time. I ordered sushi, obvi, and it was like, way smaller than what I'm used to, and the fish was sorta meh?? Like not super fresh?? AT LEAST PRETEND TO TRY, right?

The service was alright, I guess, but dude serving us looked like he wanted to be anywhere but there??? Not cool. I swear it took forever to get our check too, we pretty much had to chase the guy down lol. Also, side note: watch out for that soup, it was like SALTy AF, like did they spill the ocean in it or what?! 🤷‍♀️

Overall, just not what I expected for what I paid...would prob look elsewhere next time I'm craving sushi, y'know?

OMG, y'all, I just had the most AMAZING dinner at this lil Thai spot in Seattle, like wow. Seriously, every bite felt like a party in my mouth, no joke. We ordered the Pad Thai and it was so freakin' good, the noodles were like perfectly chewy and the sauce was just the right amt of sweet and spicy (got that little kick, u know?). Also, the Green Curry slapped, so creamy and flavorful, like I could bathe in it... is that weird? LOL. Plus, the staff was super chill and friendly, felt like I was visiting friends, not just dining out. Also, bonus points for the cute decor and the vibey setting, makes it feel kinda cozy n intimate. 🤗 If ur in Seattle and want bomb Thai food, you HAVE to check this place out, trust me, u won't regret it!! So glad we found it. ✌️

OMG this place was so delish!! 😍 Didn't know what half the stuff was but the bulgogi was legit amazing. Service was kinda slow but they were super nice, so it’s chill. Plus that kimchi tho, like, whoa! Definitely coming back 4 sure, maybe when it’s less crowded.

So like this place was okay, I guess. Went here with some friends last week. It’s called umm... Shogun Sushi or something? Tbh the name kinda got lost in my brain, lol. Sushi was decent, not mind-blowing but defs fresh. Portions were a bit small for what you pay tho.

I had the spicy tuna roll and the miso soup. The roll was like alright, but kinda bland?? I dunno, I just expected more flavor? Also, the soup was hot AF 🔥, so like warning if ur not into that lol. My friend tried the ramen and he said it was "meh" compared to what we had at that other spot, u know, that hidden gem in Chinatown, Ramen Goku or smth. That's the place to be if ur into ramen, for sure.
