Anyway, Shogun’s vibe is nice. Props for the decor - all zen n stuff, like those uh paper lanterns all over the place. Staff was chill, but our waiter seemed a bit distracted. We had to ask twice for more wasabi, the struggle is real 😅.

Parking was also a pain, typical Boston stuff I guess. Would I go back? maybe, if someone else really wants to, ya know. Otherwise, I'd probs hit up Ramen Goku again if I'm in the area. Just my two cents.

So like, went to this Indian place last night with a couple of my friends and idk, it was kinda just meh? 😳 We were all super pumped cause like, Indian food rocks, but this spot was just okay? Like, don't get me wrong, food was decent but it wasn’t, you know, blow-my-mind AMAZING. 

We got the chicken tikka masala and tbh it was pretty good but I’ve had better, like, it wasnt really spicy or anything, my friend Rob—who basically lives for spice—was kinda bummed lol. The naan was pretty solid tho, I'll give it that, nice and fluffy. But dude, service was kinda slow? Maybe they were just like under-staffed or something but felt like we waited forever for our food and for refills on water, I mean, it wasn't even crazy crowded or anything 🙄.

Ambience was nice-ish, a bit loud but I guess it's NYC so... 🤷‍♂️. So, overall, just 3 stars from me, would come back if someone else was rly set on it but probs not my first choice, ya know?

So me and my friends ended up at this seafood place in Austin cuz, like, one of them was craving fish tacos or something, also we ran into it by accident after getting lost (totally my fault lol). The place looked pretty cool from the outside, like those seaside shacks you see in movies, but Austin.

Anyway, we get in and they're kinda busy so we had to wait like 20 mins to even get seated, oh well. The interior was a bit cramped. Kinda felt like we were all on a boat ride but not in a good way? Music was okay tho, gave off a chill vibe.

I ordered the crab cakes and my friend got the fish tacos (obvs) and while the crab cakes were pretty decent, kinda tasted fresh, but no wow factor, y'know? Oh, and I tried a bite of my friend's taco... it was alright, maybe a bit too much sauce tho. Another friend ordered clam chowder, but it was like, soupy and kind of bland? Not sure if that's a word to describe food but like you get me lol.

Service was alright considering how swamped they were but tbh it was hard to get refill on water without waving our hands like crazy ppl and the bathrooms ugh! one of them was out of paper towel which drove me nuts. 

All in all, a super okay experience — nothing we'd like rush back to or tell everyone about but if ur in the area and needing a

Ugh, so me n my buddies wanted to try this new Thai spot in Denver cus we heard good things but omg was it a letdown. First off, the service was like practically non-existent, we legit waited forever just to get water. I mean how hard is it to bring some H2O?? The place wasn’t even that busy. 

When the food finally came, it did not hit the spot. My pad thai was a gloopy mess, too sweet like they poured syrup in it or somethin, and my friend’s green curry was like eating a bowl of spicy water?? I dunno if they forgot to put coconut milk in it, but yeah, it was like a soup from a sad day. Also, the portions were tiny af for the price, like why am I paying $$$ for what feels like a kid’s meal??

Plus they kept getting our orders mixed up, and one of my friends was vegan and they brought out chicken in their dish TWICE. How many times do you gotta say “no meat” until they get it? SMH. Overall, bad vibes and worse food. Can’t believe I wasted a night here, defs not coming back ever.

So like 5 of us rolled into this place last Friday, and OMG, I legit have to rave about it for a sec!! 😍 We hit up this spot w/ literally zero expectations, but wow, were we blown away or what?! First off - the VIBES. It's got this cool laid-back, sort of rustic-chic kind of ambiance, ya know? Music's not too loud, which is perf for chatting and catching up on all the drama, heh.

K, let's talk FOOD. I got the cheeseburger and it was legit one of the juiciest, most flavor-packed burgers I've ever had. The fries were totally on point too, crispy AF and seasoned just right. My friend Sarah got the BBQ ribs and was basically in heaven, she like wouldn’t shut up about how tender they were LOL. We all kinda ended up sharing everything bc honestly, everything looked sooooo good.

Service was suuuuper chill too, the staff was friendly and didn’t rush us at all even tho it was pretty crowded. Shoutout to the waitress who was p cool and recommended some dope craft beers. 🍻

TL;DR: loved it, would def hit up again if I’m ever in LA. Bringin’ more friends next time for sure 🙌.

So I went to this place with a couple friends last weekend and wow, what a disappointment. Honestly, if I could give zero stars I would. First off, the wait was RIDIC, we waited like an hour even tho they told us 20 mins. Not cool. Then the server had serious attiTude, like chill we just wanna eat not deal with your bad day.

Okay so the food, OMG, like how do you mess up a burger? It was so dry I needed like 3 drinks just to swallow it lol. And the fries were soggy AF, like ew. My friend ordered the ribs and they were so overcooked it was like chewing on wood. Seriously I'm not a picky eater but COME ON. Also, it was way overpriced like I'm not made of money here.

We ended up just leaving hungry and went for tacos instead. Will NOT come back here ever. Austin has too many good spots to waste my time on this place tbh. 😤

So I went to this seafood place in Chicago with a couple of my buddies last weekend, cuz like, we were all craving some good seafood, right? Looked promising from outside, but man, talk about a letdown. 😞 First off, idk if they were just having an off night or what, but service was suuuper slow. We were sitting there like forever before the waiter even noticed us.

Then, when the food actually came, it was... meh. My friend ordered the shrimp pasta and he said it was kinda bland, like it just tasted like noodles and some off shrimp. I got the fish tacos, and ngl, they were pretty dry and flavorless.

We did get some calamari to start off and that was actually decent, so I'll give them that. Oh, and drinks were okay but nothing special. But honestly, for the price we paid, I expected way better. We left feeling kinda bummed and still a bit hungry, not gonna lie. Wouldn't really recommend unless you don't really care what you eat. 😕

Ok, so like I was rlly excited to try this place b/c my friend said it was like REALLY good, but like idk what happened my experience was NOT IT. First off, waited like 45 mins just to get in, and ok i get it, places r busy on a Friday nite, fine. But when we finally got our table, the waiter... idr his name, Jason or Jordan or something, he seemed totally lost and like annoyingly rushed us.

So I ordered the Lobster Mac n Cheese bc, ya know, why not? But OMG, guys, it was like a MOUNTAIN of noodles with maybeeeeee like 3 pieces of lobster in it?? And they were so tiny, like little lobster ghosts, they're prolly still in the kitchen lookin for the rest of the lobster, ha. Also it tasted kinda bland, like maybe they forgot the salt or cheese or both?? 🤷‍♀️

Tried to ask for extra lemon slices just to give it SOMETHING and legit had to wave my arms for like ever to get attention from anyone, even got a cramp lol. Finally got the bill and ya girl dished out $$$ for this disappointment, not cool man, not cool. Like idk maybe I caught them on a bad night but not sure I'm risking it again. Peace out.✌️

So went here last night, and like tbh, the sushi was just meh. I mean for the price, expected better. Service was kinda slow and they forgot one of our orders. Ambience was nice tho, liked the decor. Probably won't rush back any time soon. :/

Came here last weekend with a couple buddies. We kinda heard mixed things abt this place but figured why not give it a shot. So like the vibes were chill and the staff pretty friendly, but idk the food was just okay. 

We got the sushi platter and honestly, it was kinda average. Like it wasn't bad but also nothing to write home about 😅. The fish was fresh but the rolls seemed a bit sloppy and, like, not very flavorful? The miso soup tho, I'll give them props for that, it was on point.

Oh, and my friend got the ramen and he was kinda disappointed cuz the broth was a bit bland. It's not horrible and the place was busy, so maybe we just caught them on an off night? 

I mean if ur in the area it’s worth a try but tbh not sure if I'd go outta my way to come back.

So I went to this Italian place w/ a couple of friends the other night and like, I was kinda excited bc who doesn't love pasta? But man, it was like...a letdown tbh. The vibes were okay I guess? But nothin special, and it was actually pretty noisy which made it kinda hard to like, hear each other talk.  

Anyway, we ordered a few different dishes to share and I gotta say, the pasta was just meh, like not bad but not great? Just kinda bland and like, basic saucy 🤷‍♂️. I think Olive Garden prob does it better lol. One of my friends got the pizza and it was super soggy in the middle??? Like, they gotta check the oven settings or somethin.

Service-wise, the waiter was friendly, but super slow. We waited ages for refills and they forgot one of our appetizers. When it finally came, it wasn't worth the wait ngl. Alsooo, for the price, I feel like we could've gone literally anywhere in NYC and got way better food. 

I guess if ur like in the area and desperate for food, maybe give it a shot, but otherwise, better Italian joints to hit up. 🍝

Ok so lemme just start with the fact that I don't usually write reviews but like, omg this place kinda deserves it? So I went with my bff last friday night to Ferrelinas (I hope I'm spelling that right lol) in LA and it was pretty dope tbh. The vibe was super chill and made it feel like a nice lil escape from the craziness of the city, yknow? 

We ordered the Carnitas Tacos and dude, those things were seriously on point. Like, the meat was hella juicy and just perfectly seasoned. I was low-key living for their homemade salsa too - spicy but like, it's a good kinda burn, right? Our waiter, Jesse I think his name was?, was super nice and even recommended the horchata, which btw was craaazy delicious. It's that kinda drink that makes you wanna sip really slow to make it last as long as possible.

Only reason I didn’t give it 5 stars is 'cause our food took a lil longer to get out than we thought it would, but maybe it was busy or something? Who knows. Anyway, defs gotta try this place if you're around!

Okay so like I went to this Korean spot in LA, can't remember the exact name but you can't miss it if you're around K-Town. First off, the vibe was kinda underwhelming, like you walk in expecting some major authentic feels and it's just like... meh. Service was okay, the dude serving us seemed kinda rushed but hey, who isn't these days, right? 

We ordered like the usual suspects—bibimbap, bulgogi, the works. Food was, honestly, just alright? The bulgogi didn't exactly knock my socks off, ya know? Plus, why was it kinda fatty?? Am I the only one who's like super picky about that? I dunno, could've used more flavor or something. But thank goodness for the banchan, that stuff is always like the real MVP.

Also, whyyyy was it so loud?? I get people are having a good time but I was practically shouting at my friend across the table lol. Prices are okay for LA but also like, def not the best bang for my buck, imo. Would I go back? Maaaaaybe if my friends are dragging me but I'm not running back anytime soon.

So let me tell you guys about this wild night out in Miami. My friend Laura and I were just walking around looking for a place to chill, and we kinda just stumbled upon this place, totally by accident. This little Mexican spot was like, out of nowhere and omg we were SO hungry, and dude, did it hit the spot.

First off, the vibes here are like on point. The decor is all these cool colors and there's like these little lights everywhere, totally made us feel like we were on some mini vacay. 😊

We started with some margaritas cause honestly, when in Miami haha, and wow, they were like so good. Laura got the spicy mango one which was epic. Like it had the right level of kick without setting your mouth on fire, you know?

For food, I got the tacos al pastor and like... I'm honestly crying just thinking about them. The meat was super juicy and full of that yummy flavor, and ugh the pineapple was actually perfect. Laura's enchiladas were also bomb, and she was legit just sitting there having like a moment with her food haha.

Also had to give a shoutout to the staff, who were super sweet and checked on us like a bunch of times. We felt so taken care of. 🙌

All in all, if you're in Miami and you want amazing Mexican food, you gotta hit up this place. I'm def coming back next time I'm in town, like no question. 5

Okay, so I went to "Gone Fishin’" the other night and honestly, it was kinda meh. Like, not the worst but def not what I was hoping for. I ordered the "Cajun Shrimp Platter" bc, idk, I thought it'll be spicy n flavorful or whatever, but like, it was pretty bland? Almost tasted like they forgot the cajun part... it was just shrimp with a lil’ kick but not in a good way, ya know?

And oh man, our waiter Joe was super nice but kinda all over the place. Kept mixing up drinks at our table. I asked for a Diet Coke and he brought a Sprite?? Haha like c'mon man not even close. Felt like he had too many tables to manage or something but like... that ain't our problem, right?

Anyways, atmosphere was alright, kinda loud but I guess that's normal. But overall, not really what I would call a great dining experience. Maybe it was just one of those off nights, idk. 😕 Might give it another shot, but def not on my priority list rn.

Ugh where do I even start with this place. So I went there last Saturday w my friends excited to try it out cuz like everybody’s been raving about Korean food lately. We ordered the bibimbap which is supposed to be this delicious bowl with like rice n veggies n meat? But omg it came out looking totally sad and like dry af. The egg was like weird half cooked?? Not even in a good way. 

Then the service, lol wow. Our waiter, idk think his name was Tim or something, was like not interested? We asked for extra kimchi and it literally never came. Maybe he forgot or just didn’t care idk. Also took him forever to refill our water and the place wasn't even that busy?? 

There was some kinda weird smell in there too, kinda fishy??? Honestly, def not going back. Save ur money peeps, Boston has way better places. 🤦‍♀️

okay so we went here last weekend bc my coworker would not stop raving about it and honestly... its just fine? like not bad but definitely not worth the hype lol. we got the spicy tuna roll and it was kinda bland tbh, like where was the actual spice?? and the california roll literally tasted like it was made this morning not fresh at all. 

our server kevin was nice enough i guess but took forever to bring the check and we had to ask like 3 times. also they were out of sake which like okay fair but then they didnt even tell us, we had to ask. the ramen was decent i'll give them that but its literally just noodles in broth its not rocket science lol. prices are kinda steep too for what u get imo. might go back sometime but theres definitely better japanese spots in nashville tbh

ok so we went here last weekend cuz my coworker wouldn't shut up about their pad thai and like... i dont know man. the food took FOREVER to come out and we were literally waiting like 45 mins for appetizers?? and when they finally came the spring rolls were kinda cold lol. my pad thai was decent i guess but it was like super salty?? like i could barely taste anything but salt. my bf got the green curry and he said it was too spicy but also flavorless which doesnt even make sense but thats what he said lmao. the waiter was nice enough but seemed like he forgot about us half the time. also why is everything so expensive here?? we spent like $80 for two people and the portions aren't even that big tbh. maybe it was just an off night but i'm not in a rush to come back. there's literally so many other thai places in la that do it better honestly. would only recommend if ur rly desperate i guess

went here on a saturday night and honestly it was kinda underwhelming?? like we waited like 45 mins for a table even tho we had a reservation which was annoying. anyway our waiter jake was nice enough i guess but he forgot to bring our drinks for like 20 mins and we had to ask twice lol

ordered the burger and fries and it was just... fine? the meat was kinda dry and the bun was sorta soggy from the sauce. fries were decent at least but nothing special. my girlfriend got the chicken sandwich and she said it tasted like it came out of a freezer tbh

the prices are actually pretty high for what you get. like we spent almost 60 bucks and didnt leave feeling like it was worth it. the place looks nice inside i'll give them that but if the food isnt good then what's the point ya know?? might try somewhere else next time

ok so i went here last weekend with my coworkers and honestly it was pretty solid?? like the banchan situation was A+ unlimited side dishes r the way to go ngl. the bibimbap was actually insane like the rice got a little crispy on the bottom which some ppl dont like but i think thats the best part lol. my only thing is our server took kinda long to come back and we had to flag her down for more water like twice which wasnt ideal but she was nice when she did show up so whatever. the bulgogi was also really good, super tender and flavorful. prices r reasonable for portland which is like basically impossible these days so thats cool. would definitely go back tbh even tho the ambiance is kinda loud?? but thats like every place downtown these days. def recommend if ur craving korean food and dont wanna spend a million dollars

ok so we went here bc my coworker said it was good but honestly like it was just eh?? the pad thai was kinda bland and needed way more fish sauce or soy sauce or like SOMETHING lol. and it took forever to get our food out even tho it wasnt even that busy. we waited like 45 mins for 3 entrees which is kind of ridiculous tbh

the curry had a weird aftertaste too?? idk it was just off. i went to this other place on calle ocho last month and their pad thai actually tasted like pad thai u know what i mean. it had flavor and everything 

service was ok i guess, not rude but also not great. waiter forgot to bring napkins and water refills. the ambiance is nice tho if ur into that fairy light vibe lol

idk maybe we just caught them on a bad day but id rather go back to the spot on calle ocho honestly. wont be returning

honestly this place slaps?? came here w my friends last sat and got the burger n fries and like it was actually SO good. the fries were crispy af and the burger had mad flavor. service was pretty quick too. only thing is it got kinda loud in there and our server forgot to bring our drinks at first but whatever lol would def come back

ugh honestly worst korean food ive had in the city and i go to korean town like every other week. ordered the bibimbap and it was literally cold?? like not even room temp, COLD cold. and the rice was all mushy which like defeats the whole purpose. the banchan were clearly from yesterday or something bc they tasted stale af. their side dishes at han ah reum down the street are 10x better and cheaper too.

server was nice i guess but that doesnt make up for the fact that we waited 45 mins for two entrees. place was barely full!! also they charged us for extra kimchi when literally every other korean spot gives you unlimited. wont be back tbh, total waste of money and time. even the gogi house around the corner is way better and its not even that close

ok so my girlfriend was rly excited about this place cuz her coworker kept saying how good it was and we finally went last saturday night. like we waited like 20 mins even tho we had a reservation which was already annoying lol. 

anyway the pasta was just like... fine? idk it tasted kinda generic honestly. i got the fettucini alfredo and it was way too heavy and the sauce didnt have much flavor, just like heavy cream basically. my gf got the lasagna and she said it was dry?? like the noodles were kinda hard which was weird. 

the waiter was nice at least and the breadsticks were decent but tbh for what we paid ($65 total before tip) i expected way more. the place itself is cute and the vibe is okay but if the foods not good whats the point ya know. we probably wont go back unless they really step it up. theres way better italian places around seattle that dont overcharge you

went here with my friends last saturday and honestly it was like... fine? the pad thai was actually really good not gonna lie, nice and spicy just how i like it. BUT the service was kinda slow even tho it wasnt that busy?? we waited like 20 mins just to order drinks lol. 

the curry i got was decent but i feel like ive had better tbh. and they were kind of stingy with the rice which is annoying when ur paying 16 bucks for a plate. the mango sticky rice at the end was SOLID tho that made up for some stuff. 

also the bathroom was pretty gross which is never a good sign for a restaurant ngl. would probably come back if im in the area but id maybe try some other spots first. its not bad its just like... nothing special yk?? def hit or miss depending on what u order i guess

so my coworker literally would NOT stop talking about this place for like weeks right?? kept saying their butter chicken was "life changing" lol ok whatever. finally dragged my bf there on a friday night and honestly it was just... mid??

the ambiance was nice i guess, kinda dark and moody but idk felt like the music was way too loud. had to basically yell at each other the whole time which is annoying when ur trying to have a conversation. 

ok so the butter chicken - and i hate to be that person but it was definitely NOT what ppl were hyping up. like it was sweet?? too sweet?? and kinda watery honestly. the naan was actually pretty decent, cant complain about that. but my bf got the biryani and he said it was underseasoned and there wasnt even that much chicken in it??? for the price thats kinda wack

the server was nice enough but took forever to come back after we ordered. like we waited like 20 mins just to place our order and then another 45 for the food. wasnt even that busy so idk what was going on

overall its not bad if ur desperate and in the neighborhood but there are def better indian places in the city. would not go back tbh. maybe it was just an off night but idk doesnt seem worth the hype or the prices. save

ok so i went here last weekend cuz my friend wouldnt shut up about their kimchi jjigae and like... its good? dont get me wrong the broth is literally SO flavorful and the kimchi has that perfect kick to it. but honestly the service was kinda slow even tho it wasnt even that busy lol. our server forgot to bring us water for like 15 mins and then when the food came out it wasnt hot enough which is weird for a stew??? 

the banchan selection is LEGIT tho, i counted like 8 different sides and they keep refilling them which is dope. the price is reasonable too which i appreciate. i got the galbi and it was tender AF but also like... not the most memorable? kind of forgettable seasoning ngl

would i come back? yeah probably if someone drags me here again lol. its a solid spot but definitely not gonna be making a special trip or anything. not bad just not AMAZING you know?? go if youre in the area i guess

omg this place is so good like i literally come here like twice a month now and i still cant decide what to order?? the pad thai is insane honestly better than anywhere else ive been in austin and ive tried a LOT of thai places lol. the curry is also so flavorful and like the portion sizes are actually huge for the price which is clutch. the staff is super nice too even when im being annoying and asking a million questions about the menu lmaooo. ngl their mango sticky rice hits different and i always get it for dessert. only thing is sometimes the wait can get long on weekends but tbh its worth it bc the food is that good. if youre in austin and havent been here yet what are u even doing?? go now 10/10

honestly this place was such a disappointment like we went on a saturday night and the waiter carlos was just nowhere to be found the whole time. we had to flag someone else down just to get water refills. anyway we ordered the risotto and it was like... mushy? like not in a good way, it tasted kinda old ngl. my boyfriends pasta carbonara had this weird cream sauce that def isnt supposed to be in carbonara lol and the noodles were all stuck together. spent like $70 for two entrees and we were both still hungry after. there are so many better italian spots in the area that actually care about their food. not going back thats for sure

came here w my friends last saturday for my bday and honestly it was kinda underwhelming?? like the place looks nice and everything but the food was just meh. got the salmon and it was dry af, like i dont even know how u mess that up lol. my friend got the crab cakes and she said they were good but like also her standards r pretty low so idk. 

the waiter was nice tho thats the only reason its not one star. our drinks took forever to come out and then we had to ask for water like three times??? and dont even get me started on the prices bc paying $28 for dry salmon is honestly criminal. 

also there was this weird fishy smell that was kinda off putting but maybe thats just what seafood restaurants smell like idk. would prolly not come back unless i have no other options. theres like a million other places in the city that do seafood way better. sucks bc it seemed promising from the reviews

ok so my family wanted to try this place bc my mom saw it on instagram and literally everyone was hyping it up. we went on a saturday night and honestly it was such a disappointment lol. 

first of all we waited like 45 mins for a table even tho we had a reservation?? the hostess kept saying "just a few more minutes" and then we finally sat down. then we waited another 20 mins just to get water lmao.

the food was honestly kinda mid. my dad got the butter chicken and he said it tasted like it was from a can or something, like literally no flavor. my naan was cold when it got to the table and my sister's samosas were greasy as hell. we're not picky people but this was just bad.

the service was terrible too. our waiter disappeared for like 30 mins after we ordered and when we tried to flag someone down they were just standing around talking. and dont even get me started on the bill - they charged us for stuff we didn't even order.

wont be going back tbh. theres so many better indian places in miami that actually care about their customers. save your money!!

ugh okay so we went here last weekend cuz my friend wouldn't stop talking about it and honestly?? kinda disappointed ngl. the brisket was like dry af and we had to ask for extra sauce just to get thru it lol. the sides were fine i guess but nothing special, mac n cheese tasted like it came from a box or somethin. 

what really got me was how long we waited for our food like we ordered and literally sat there for 45 mins while other ppl got their stuff way faster?? the place wasnt even that busy so idk what was going on. waiter didnt really check on us either which was annoying.

the vibes are chill i'll give them that and the outdoor seating is nice but like you can get way better bbq for the same price honestly. probably won't come back unless they seriously step up their game. maybe they were just having an off day but yeah not worth the hype for us

ok so i went here with my boyfriend last saturday and honestly it was such a waste of money lol. we got the fettuccine alfredo and it was literally like eating a bowl of butter?? like the sauce was SO heavy and the noodles were kinda mushy??? idk how you mess that up its literally just pasta and cream lmao

and dont even get me started on our waiter marco. he forgot our water refills like three times and when we asked for the check it took him literally 20 mins to bring it. he seemed more interested in chatting with other servers than actually helping us 😒

the tiramisu was dry af and tasted like it was from last week. price was also ridic for the quality we got. theres so many better italian places in la that actually know what theyre doing. honestly wont be going back and wouldnt recommend this place to anyone sorry

ok so we went here last saturday bc my coworker wouldnt stop talking about their butter chicken and like... it was fine? not bad but def not worth the hype lol. the food took forever to come out and we were literally the only people there which was kinda weird. 

the butter chicken was SWIMMING in sauce like i couldnt even taste the chicken and the naan was kinda hard?? should be soft and fluffy not like cardboard lmao. my friend got the saag paneer and said it was too salty. 

the waiter was nice enough i guess but kinda awkward and he forgot our water refills. also their mango lassi tasted like it was from a mix not fresh or anything. the place smells pretty good i'll give them that but the prices are kinda high for the quality honestly. 

wouldnt say dont go but DEFINITELY dont expect miracles. there's better indian spots in the city for sure. maybe they were just having an off night but yeah idk seems overrated to me 🤷

honestly this place was such a letdown lol. we went for our anniversary and it was literally one of the worst meals ive had in denver. the "homemade" pasta was like mushy and overcooked?? idk how you mess that up that bad. and dont even get me started on the sauce - it tasted like they just opened a jar of ragù from walmart or something. 

our server was nice but took FOREVER to bring our food like we waited almost 45 mins for two entrees. the ambiance was okay i guess but it was pretty loud and awkward vibes overall. plus they charged us $18 for a glass of wine that tasted like vinegar fr fr. 

we could've gone to literally any other italian place in denver and had a better experience ngl. honestly gonna stick to nonna's or that other place on 17th. dont waste your money here unless you like bland overpriced pasta tbh

ok so we went here on a saturday nite and honestly the vibes were immaculate like the place was packed but in a good way ya know?? the carne asada tacos were so good i literally had like 4 of them lol and the guac was fresh af. my only thing is why does every mexican restaurant in miami charge $6 for a margarita when they taste like straight up mix?? like this one wasnt terrible but it wasnt worth the price tag tbh

the service was rly good tho our waiter was super attentive and kept the chips coming which honestly kept me from being annoyed about the wait for our food. the chile rellenos were a little dry but the enchiladas verdes made up for it. def coming back but maybe ill just stick to beer next time lol

ok so we went here last saturday and honestly the brisket was like... ACTUALLY good?? ive been to a bunch of bbq places around denver and this place actually does it right. the bark was crispy and the meat just fell apart which is what ur supposed to get ya know. sides were solid too, the mac n cheese had that creamy thing going on and wasnt dry which is my biggest pet peeve lol.

only thing is the wait was kinda long?? like we got there around 6 and didnt sit down til like 6:45 but i guess thats normal for saturdays. also the ribs were a little less impressive than the brisket tbh, they were good but not like life changing or anything. 

the staff was super nice tho and the sauce is ACTUALLY homemade which i respect. worth going back for sure, would def recommend if ur craving decent bbq without having to drive super far. just maybe go on a weekday lol

went here last weekend on a friends recommendation and honestly it was pretty mid. like the naan was ACTUALLY good, like super fluffy and had that perfect char on it, but then the chicken tikka masala tasted kinda like it had been sitting under a heat lamp for way too long?? idk it was just like flavorless which is crazy bc usually this place gets recs. also we waited like 20 mins for our food and it wasnt even that busy which was annoying. the mango lassi was solid tho i'll give em that. the staff seemed nice enough but our waiter forgot to bring our rice for like half the meal. would maybe come back if theyre having an off day but theres def better spots in the city for indian food imo. not bad just not worth the hype or the prices tbh

omg this place is SO good!! had the burger and fries and like honestly it was perfect. the staff was super nice and didnt rush us at all even tho we were there forever lol. prices are decent too which is rare in chicago ngl. definitely coming back asap!! would 100% recommend to anyone in the area

ugh ok so i went here last week bc my coworker wouldnt stop talking abt their pad thai and honestly?? i was so disappointed lol. like the food took forever to come out and when it did the noodles were kinda mushy??? and i specifically asked for medium spice but it was literally like eating a mild salsa it had NO kick at all. my boyfriend got the green curry and he said it tasted like they forgot to add the actual curry paste or whatever. the waiter was nice tho i'll give them that but like nice service doesn't fix mushy noodles ya know?? the place is also super loud and cramped which was annoying when we just wanted a chill dinner. prices are not cheap either so i feel like we def overpaid for what we got. maybe they just had an off night?? idk but im prob not rushing back unless someone shows me their cooking actually improved bc this aint it chief 😬

ok so we went here last saturday and it was like... fine? the pad thai was actually really good, not gonna lie, my bf got that and he kept stealing from my plate lol. my green curry was a little watery tho like the coconut flavor wasnt as strong as i wanted it to be. our waiter marco was super nice and attentive which was cool, he remembered we wanted extra basil without us having to ask twice.

the place itself is kinda cramped and loud but i guess thats what you expect in boston right? prices are reasonable which i appreciate. my only real complaint besides the curry is that the spring rolls came out cold?? like they mustve been sitting in the kitchen for a min. they tasted ok but def better hot lol

would prob come back if we're in the area but its not like a destination place for us. decent spot if your craving thai and dont wanna go too far out of your way tbh

went here last weekend bc my friend wouldn't stop talking abt it and honestly it was just... fine? like the hummus was good but the lamb was kinda dry and our server forgot our drinks for like 20 mins. prices are def not worth it for what u get. there's better mediterranean spots in the gulch imo

omg so my roommate wouldn't stop raving about this place and finally dragged me and like 3 other friends there last saturday night and honestly?? it was SO good. we got the pad thai, some curry thing (idk which one but it was red and spicy lol), spring rolls, and mango sticky rice for dessert. literally everything was amazing and not even that expensive?? the pad thai had the perfect amount of like crunch and the noodles were cooked perfectly. and the curry was actually making me sweat but in like a good way haha. our server was super nice too even tho we were kinda loud and annoying. definitely gonna go back asap probably this weekend lol. if you like thai food and you live in chicago you HAVE to check this out fr fr

honestly this place is such a letdown. we went for our anniversary and like the waiter got our order wrong TWICE and then acted like it was our fault?? the pasta was literally overcooked and mushy, i dont even know how u mess up spaghetti thats like basic stuff. and dont even get me started on the sauce it tasted like it came out of a jar from the grocery store. we paid $80+ for two entrees and it was worse than what i can make at home in 20 mins so like why would i ever come back. the place is super loud too so we couldnt even have a nice conversation. the only good thing was the bread but like thats not hard to get right. honestly save ur money and go literally anywhere else in seattle there are so many better italian restaurants

ok so went here w my friends last saturday and honestly the bibimbap was FIRE like actually so good. the rice was crispy on the bottom which is apparently a thing and i didnt even know that but it was amazing. our waiter marco was super helpful too he didnt judge us when we asked a bunch of dumb questions about what was spicy lol

only thing is we waited like 45 mins for a table even tho we had a reservation?? kinda annoying ngl. and the banchan refills were a lil slow but whatever not a huge deal. the tteokbokki was also pretty good tho the sauce was kinda sweet for my taste but my roommate loved it so 🤷

def going back when im in that area again. prices r pretty reasonable for how good the food is. would recommend if ur craving korean

omg this place is INSANE. went there last saturday w some friends and literally cannot stop thinking about the brisket. like it was so tender it just fell apart?? and dont even get me started on the sauce - perfectly balanced, not too sweet, not too vinegary, just RIGHT. 

the ribs were also incredible, got the dry rub and honestly theyre probably the best ive had in the city. the sides were all amazing too - mac n cheese was creamy af and the cornbread had the perfect amount of sweetness. we got the burnt ends as an appetizer and they were gone in like 2 seconds lmao

service was pretty quick even tho it was busy, staff was super friendly and helpful with recommendations. the place itself is kinda casual which i actually love, way less pretentious than some of the other bbq spots around here. prices are fair for the quality tbh, def not breaking the bank.

only tiny complaint is we wished there was more seating inside but thats pretty minor. literally cannot wait to go back and try their pulled pork!! if you live in chicago and havent been here yet youre seriously missing out. 10/10 would recommend to literally anyone

ok so we went here last saturday and honestly it was pretty good!! the burgers were REALLY juicy like way better than i expected for the price. my bf got the classic cheeseburger and he said it was legit one of the best he's had in the city which is saying something lol. i got their chicken sandwich and it was crispy on the outside but still moist inside so that was clutch

the fries were a lil salty for my taste but my friend didnt mind hers so maybe just depends. service was decent, our waiter was friendly even tho it was kinda busy when we got there. only thing that bugged me was we waited like 20 mins for a table even tho we had a reservation?? that was weird but once we sat down everything moved pretty quick so whatever

the place has rly cute retro vibes too, like the booths are comfortable and the music wasnt too loud which is nice. prices are super reasonable tbh, like for what you get it's def worth it. would probs come back again, just maybe without the family this time bc my cousin was being annoying lmaoo

if ur looking for solid american food thats not gonna break the bank this spot hits different 👍

omg so i went here last weekend with my bf and honestly it was rly good!! like the mezze platter was absolutely fire, had the hummus, baba ganoush, all that good stuff and the pita was still warm which like... u wouldnt think thats important but it changes everything lol. the lamb kebabs were so tender and flavorful i was shook. 

BUT and this is why its not 5 stars... service was kinda slow?? like we waited forever for our drinks and then forever to order and it took like an hour for our food to come out. guy at the table next to us got his food like 20 mins before us even tho they ordered after us so idk what was going on there 😅 

also the prices are getting a little crazy ngl but i guess thats miami for u. would deff go back tho the food is legit worth it. they need to get their timing together tho fr fr

ok so i went here on a saturday night bc my friend wouldnt shut up about their pasta and honestly it was the biggest disappointment ever?? like we waited almost 45 mins for a table even tho we had a reservation which is already annoying. then when the food finally came my spaghetti was literally cold in the middle. COLD. and dont even get me started on the sauce, it tasted like it came straight out of a jar from the grocery store. my boyfriends risotto was mushy and flavorless lol. our server barely checked on us the whole time and when we mentioned the cold pasta he was kinda rude abt it?? like not our fault dude. we paid like $60 per person for mediocre food and bad service. theres literally so many better italian places in the city. would not recommend at all, such a waste of money and time tbh

honestly we were so hyped to try this place bc our coworkers kept raving about it but like... it was kinda mid??? came here with my gf and another couple on a saturday night and we waited forever for a table even tho we had a reservation lol. then when the food finally came out the bulgogi was kinda dry and the banchan situation was not it - like we got maybe 3 sides when other tables seemed to have way more?? 

the kimchi fried rice was actually pretty good tho and my gf's dakbokkeumtang was solid so thats why im not giving it 1 star but tbh for the prices and the wait it just wasnt worth the hype. plus our server forgot to bring water like multiple times and seemed kinda annoyed when we asked for refills. might give it another shot sometime but def wont be rushing back anytime soon. there are so many other korean spots in austin that are way better imo

omg this place is SO good like we went on a random tuesday and the lobster roll was literally the best ive ever had?? the fries were crispy and their clam chowder is insane ngl. service was super friendly too even tho we were late. def coming back asap this is now my fav spot in the city hands down

ugh ok so we went here bc my coworker wouldnt stop raving about their biryani and honestly i should have just listened to my gut. first of all we waited like 20 mins before a waiter named raj even came to take our drink order?? and the place wasnt even that busy. then when our food finally came the chicken tikka masala was just... bland? like straight up had no flavor i literally added a ton of salt and it still tasted like nothing. my bf got the biryani everyone was hyping up and it was all soggy at the bottom which is kinda gross. not to mention our bill was way overpriced for the quality lol. there are so many better places on this street honestly dont waste ur time here unless you like mediocre food for expensive prices tbh. probably wont be back

ok so we went here for our anniversary last saturday and honestly it was such a disappointment. the pasta was like mushy??? i dont know how u mess up pasta this bad lol. my boyfriend ordered the lasagna and he said it tasted like it came from a frozen dinner box fr fr. the sauce was SO bland like we had to add like half the salt shaker just to make it edible. also our server forgot our drinks for like 45 mins and then when she brought them they were warm?? and not like "room temp" warm i mean ACTUALLY WARM. 

the vibe was kinda nice i guess but that doesnt make up for serving bad food tbh. we spent like over $100 on this and could've gone literally anywhere else. the manager didn't even apologize when we mentioned the pasta being bad, she just kind of shrugged??? never coming back lol dont waste ur money here there r so many better italian places in denver

so my roommate wouldnt stop talking about this place and literally dragged me here last saturday for my bday dinner lol. i was like ok fine whatever but honestly kinda disappointed??

the restaurant itself is cute and the staff was super nice dont get me wrong BUT the food was just... mid? i got the chirashi bowl and it wasnt bad but like it tasted kinda bland?? my roommate got ramen and said the broth was "too salty" which like coming from her thats saying something bc she drowns everything in soy sauce lmaooo

the sushi looked pretty but some of the pieces were like falling apart when we picked them up w chopsticks which is weird. also waited like 45 mins for our food and it wasnt even that busy?? there were literally 3 other tables. the tempura appetizer we ordered was soggy by the time it came out which was kind of a bummer

prices are def on the higher side too tbh. like $18 for a bowl that tasted like it could be $12? idk maybe i just had an off experience but i wouldnt rush back anytime soon. might try it again sometime but theres way better japanese spots around seattle imo. sorry roommate 😅

ok so my roommate wouldn't stop raving about this place and i was like "yeah yeah whatever" but then i actually went and honestly it was pretty solid. we went on a saturday around 7 and it was packed but they got us seated pretty quick which was nice.

got the carnitas tacos and omg they were actually really good, like the meat was so tender and not dry at all?? my roommate got the chile rellenos and said it was the best shes had in seattle which is saying something cause she's from like actual mexico lol. the salsa is also legit, not that weird watered down stuff.

my only complaint is the service was a litttle slow towards the end like we were kinda just sitting there waiting for the check for forever. also the portions could maybe be a tiny bit bigger for the price but its seattle so like... what do you expect tbh.

honestly would def go back tho. the food is really good and they have a solid margarita list. just maybe go on a weekday if you dont wanna wait lol

omg okay so i went here last week w my coworkers and honestly the food was pretty damn good?? like the bibimbap was SO much better than the place we usually go to in wicker park, the sauce had actual flavor instead of being all watered down lol. also got the bulgogi and it was like, melting in my mouth type situation, def would get again. only thing that bugged me was we had to wait like 45 mins even tho we had a reservation?? and our server seemed kinda annoyed when we asked for extra banchan but like thats literally the whole vibe of korean restaurants so idk what his deal was lmao. the prices r a lil steep ngl but i guess you get what u pay for. would def come back, probly just gonna go during off hours to avoid the whole wait situation. 10/10 would recommend to anyone craving authentic korean food

ok so we went here last saturday and it was pretty decent? like the brisket was actually really good, nice and smoky and not dry which is honestly what i was worried about. my boyfriend got the ribs and he said they were solid too. the sides were fine, mac and cheese was kinda mid but the cornbread was pretty good lol

BUT here's the thing - we waited like 45 mins for a table even tho we called ahead?? and then our server was super slow, like it took forever to get drinks and the food came out cold the first time so we had to send it back. which honestly was annoying. also the prices are kinda EXPENSIVE for seattle bbq imo, like we spent almost $100 for just the two of us which felt like a lot

the actual bbq itself is good quality and i would prob go back but maybe not on a weekend bc the wait times seem INSANE. also their sauce is really good, i bought a bottle to take home lol. idk would recommend but just know you're gonna be waiting

ugh ok so we went there last saturday and the pad thai was just... fine? like its not bad but honestly for the price its kinda mid. the server was nice tho which was cool. my friend got the curry and she said it was too spicy even tho she asked them to go easy on it lol. might go back sometime but theres probably better places

omg finally found an AMAZING mexican place in seattle!! honestly been looking for real authentic stuff for like ever and this place totally delivers. the carnitas tacos are literally insane - so tender and flavorful?? and dont even get me started on the salsa, its homemade and has the perfect kick to it. went with my roommate last weekend and we both got the chile rellenos and like... why did we not come here sooner lmao. 

service was super friendly too, our waiter remembered our drinks without asking when he came back. prices are actually really reasonable for how good it is tbh. only minor thing is it gets kinda loud when theres a crowd but honestly thats a good sign right?? means everyone else knows its good too lol. definitely coming back next week already told like half my friend group about this place. if youre in seattle and want real mexican food PLEASE go here you wont regret it 5 stars ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

honestly this place is pretty solid? like we went on a saturday nite and didnt have to wait too long which was nice. the ramen was actually rly good, like the broth had actual flavor u know, not that bland stuff u get at some places. my bf got the tonkotsu and he was obsessed lol. 

the gyoza were crispy on the bottom which is exactly how theyre supposed to be and the edamame was seasoned perfect. only thing is the service was kinda slow in the middle of our meal like they forgot about us for a bit?? but it picked back up. also their sushi is decent but like why would u get sushi when the ramen is so good honestly. 

prices are reasonable for seattle, like not crazy expensive. would def come back. prob my go-to ramen spot now instead of the place on pike. 4 stars bc of the slow service moment but food was legit 10/10

honestly was really hyped to try this place after seeing it on insta but like... idk man. the brisket was kinda dry and chewy? which is like the ONE thing you gotta get right at a bbq place, right?? also they were out of like half their sides by 7pm on a saturday which seems crazy for a restaurant. the pulled pork wasnt terrible i guess but it tasted like it'd been sitting under the heat lamp for hours. plus the sauce was way too sweet imo, like drinking ketchup almost lol. service was fine tho our server was nice. would maybe try again if theyre having a better day but honestly theres better spots in seattle that dont charge $18 for a sandwich that isnt even that good. probably wont be back

ok so my buddy mike kept telling me i NEEDED to go to this place and i was like yeah yeah whatever but then his mom visited from out of town and we all went together and honestly it wasnt terrible lol. like the brisket was pretty solid, real tender and had good smoke on it. their sauce is like... fine? nothing crazy but it works. 

BUT and this is a big but, we waited like 45 mins for a table on a saturday and our sides took forever to come out. like we got our meat way before the sides even showed up which was kinda weird lmaooo. also their mac and cheese was lowkey dry?? idk maybe we got a bad batch or something. the pulled pork was good tho ngl.

place is pretty decent if you dont mind the wait and go in with expectations that are like... realistic. its solid bbq for seattle standards i guess but definitely not worth the hype mike was giving it lol. would maybe go back if im in the area but not driving across town for it or anything. nice staff tho they were actually pretty chill even when busy

went here w my roommate last friday and honestly it was pretty good!! we got the panang curry and the pad thai and both were actually really solid. the panang had like the perfect amount of spice?? not too crazy but def had a kick. our waiter mark was super chill and kept refilling our waters without us even asking lol

only thing is it took kinda long to get our food out like we were there for almost 2 hours but i guess they were busy. the prices are a lil high but its in west hollywood so whatevr. the place is cute too, really nice vibes. would def come back when im in the area again. just maybe go on a less busy night if u dont wanna wait forever haha

ok so we went here last weekend bc my coworker wouldnt shut up about their brisket lol. honestly it was pretty good?? like the meat was tender and falling apart which is what you want i guess. the sauce they had was a lil too sweet for my taste but whatever

our waiter kyle was super nice and got our order in fast which i appreciated bc i was STARVING. the sides were kinda mid tho ngl the mac and cheese tasted like it came out of a box and the cornbread was dry af. like why would you dry out cornbread that makes no sense

the prices were a bit steep imo for what you get, like we dropped almost 80 bucks for three people and it wasnt even that much food. also its always so loud in there, we could barely hear each other talking which was annoying. 

would i go back? maybe if someone else is paying lol. its decent bbq but theres prob better places in la that wont kill ur wallet. seems like a solid spot if ur in the area tho

omg so we went here on a saturday nite and honestly it was so good?? like we were kinda skeptical bc we've had some meh indian food around boston but this place absolutely slapped. the saag paneer was literally the best i've ever had like so creamy and the paneer was actually soft not that rubbery garbage u usually get. and dont even get me started on the naan it was warm and fluffy straight out of the tandoor or whatevr. the waiters were super nice and helpful too like they actually knew what was spicy and what wasnt which sounds dumb but its so rare lol. only thing that was kinda slow was waiting for our food but honestly it was worth it and also it was busy so like i get it. prices are reasonable too tbh thought it would be more expensive for how good it is. def going back soon prob this weekend actually lol

so my dad was visiting from out of state and kept talking about how denver has all these amazing bbq spots now and i was like okay cool lets check it out. we decided to go to this place on a saturday around 6 and honestly the wait wasnt even that bad which was surprising.

but then... idk the brisket was just kinda dry?? like it had decent flavor i guess but it fell apart in a weird way and definitely seemed like it sat under a heat lamp for too long. the ribs were better but also pretty meh, not fall-off-the-bone or anything. we got the combo platter and the pulled pork was actually the only thing that was pretty good but thats like the easiest thing to do right lol

the sides were honestly forgettable. coleslaw was just fine, beans were kinda bland, mac and cheese was weirdly gritty?? the cornbread was actually decent tho

service was chill and friendly enough and the prices arent too crazy so thats cool i guess. but for what people hype this place up to be i was kinda disappointed. my dad didnt say anything but i could tell he was underwhelmed too which is saying something bc hes usually easy to please with bbq. 

might try it again sometime but idk theres gotta be better spots in denver. would give it another shot

saw another movie & this time in the cinema suites theatre.  Wonderful experience.  the chairs recline to almost lie down & you get a free cup of chocolate covered popcorn & a wetnap.  I felt like I was flying first class :)\nagain, I have to mention the sound system- it is really impressive.  Not so impressive was the \"limited time only\" calamari appetizer - I had to send it back.
Enjoying an amazing birthday dinner with family and friends... With a sushi feast to make the gods green with jealousy!

I suppose the upside of a bad first date is that i've now tried a new restaurant to Yelp about, and I didn't even have to pay!

The atmosphere:
I really liked the look and feel of this place. There was a large patio which was quite full of folks enjoying the nice weather we have right now. We sat inside the restaurant which was not as crowded. The colors and decor are trendy, with a relaxed vibe. The music selection was very good, though much too loud. 

The food:
We started with the crab puffs. I'm not sure what the dipping sauce was that they came with but my date seemed to like it. I, however, was not a fan.  Next was the sushi. We ordered California rolls and Las Vegas rolls. Not very adventurous, I know, but they even managed to go wrong with these basics! I would have to declare these the worst sushi rolls I've ever had. 
Dessert - the saving grace. We ordered the green tea ice cream and I was surprised when they brought such a large bowl to the table! This was quite enjoyable.

While I won't be back here for sushi, I can see where this may be a good place to have a cocktail or two. Some of the selections on the drink menu sounded quite good, and very reasonable happy hour prices. I refrained from trying any though since I was with someone who doesn't drink.

I've been here 3 times for a group happy hour. 

The first time, it was excellent.  Had a great waiter who stayed on top of things and even came over to remind us about happy hour ending and to see if we wanted any more drink orders so he could get them in before the cut off.

The second time, it was a bit worse.  We had a large group, and took up the entire patio.  However, there was only 1 waitress for about 40 of us.  She forgot orders, brought out wrong things, and had no idea who ordered which thing.  She overcharged me too many drinks that had already been paid for.

But since they have decent food, and GREAT prices during happy hour, we decided to come a third time.
Apparently the manager didn't appreciate our business because he immediately asked when we would be leaving.  He said that we take up the entire patio and no one else can sit out there because we take all the seats.  Mind you, every single person there is a customer.  Every single one of us ordered multiple drinks and plenty of food.  It's not like anyone was just sitting taking a seat, but not paying for anything.
This manager was so rude, that my co-workers debated leaving right then.  We will definitely not come back again.  We also do a fund raiser with them and our school once or twice a year.  We are planning on canceling that.

They have lost our business now, which is unfortunate.


If you have a large group, go elsewhere.  If you are looking for cheap drinks, and there's only a few of you, this is an okay place.  The pretzels are cheap and okay, nothing special.

My major compliment is that they have Wyder's Pear Cider, which is my favorite!
Food was decent but not amazing while the service was pretty slow.  Atmosphere was rather stale and uninteresting so I'd recommend take-out.

When it's free, everything seems that much better. We got in on one of the LMFAO nights and it was awesome. What a way to end your vegas trip. I like the unique feel of each room, especially the library where you're not bumping elbows to get a drink. I still prefer the layout of XS, but it's always cool to see the designers of these super clubs push the boundaries of creativity and above all else, giving people a great party atmosphere.

Pizza is okay NOT THE BEST ..very rude ppl asked for a slice ..they didnt have a slice i had to buy a whole pizza..should consider taking the slices of the menu it happend one time now a second time!! Will never go bak here again!!

IF YOU ARE A HOMEOWNER AND TRYING TO USE THIS COMPANY TO DO BUSINESS............... RUN!!!!! the owner and management staff are the rudest people that you can possibly work with. They have a zero communication policy from my understanding because they will never let you know what is going on with your property unless you inquire and follow up. Everytime there is a problem you have to do most of the leg work if you want to find out any details. Not sure if the company was sold but i have been with them for 5 years and the last year has been the most disastrous i have experienced. There are numerous rental companies, id try another one prior to resorting to them.
Mikkie Finnz's sister bar next door. The food is almost as horrible.  The fish and chips suck and so does the whole menu. DO not eat here.

oh heck no!!!! don't go here!!!!!

thee most painful pedicure i ever got!!!!!!!

they cut deep into the sides of your nails. and after the guy with blond tips was done with my mani-pedi, he washed the tools with soap and cold water. that's so fricken dirty!!!!

my feet still hurt!

Worst indian food. Total waste of money. Lunch buffet is $12 with only small selection of food. Chicken is the worst I ever had. Sweet dish ( gulab jamun) very strange taste. I came with group of people, and was embarrassed to be the host. I am going back there unless they change the cook and increase selection.

Very pleased with Caitlin's Tender Love & Pet Care! It gave me great peace of mind to know that Kobe was at a "home away from home" for the weekend instead of locked up in a kennel all day. He was given attention, played with dogs all day, and even slept on the bed. Caitlin is understanding and very easy to talk to. Kobe is pretty easy going but as a puppy still has some faults -- she was wonderful with working with Kobe. She even provided a "While You Were Gone" information sheet after picking Kobe up going over his day-to-day activities. I will definitely be using Caitlin's Tender Love & Pet Care again! More cities need to have a wonderful business like this!

I also want to add that Kobe's breed -- Shiba Inu -- usually leaves him reserved around strangers, however there are certain people he immediately bonds with (I like to refer to them as "good hearted people") and Kobe loved and trusted Caitlin from the start!

Thanks again Caitlin!

I gotta say of all the fast food in cave creek, Carl's jr has been by far the best. Only issue I have is that I ordered 1 double western bacon cheeseburger for $ 4.50 and just a western bacon cheeseburger (only has one patty) for 3.99. Then I ordered 2 medium French fries for 2.50 each. How is it that 2 medium fries are 5.00 and the double western burger is only 4.50. Then, there's only .50 cents more for another patty??\nEither way, French fries are wayyyyyy to expensive! 2.50 seriously?? Total was 15.00 for fries and hamburgers. No, sodas. Ridiculous. \nHamburgers were great however as always but then we totally also got shorted on the French fries. Like we were there at 7 and they didn't wanna make more so they evenly distributed whatever they had left so I paid 5.00 bucks for basically 1 fry.  Wanna come back but feel ripped off.

The food is very fresh.  The service is great.  The resturant is one of our favorites.  We have been there about once a month.  It's not so easy to find.  Behind some buildings that are on Sahara.  But worth it.  Everyone I take there sayes it could be the best Shushi in America!  I went there because of the good reviews on Yelp and am glad I did.  Had Tuna, Salmon, Eel, California rolls, Halibit, Shrimp, Mushrooms, Soup, Salad and Beet sherbert.  Sake was great has Lots of Beers and so full I had to waddel out....Had seconds on Tuna and Salmon.

Tao is off the hook!  Bex hooked it up as always.  Great music, great staff, atmosphere was great.  If you guys make it out to Vegas, go to Tao..you \nwon't regret it!  Holla at my girl BEx and she will hook it up with a great deal.  Bex once again, you did it..thanks for always hooking it up!  Until next time!!

Was lazy in planning and didn't make reservations in time for anyplace better.  First time visit here for me, last Saturday night.  \n\nFood was just okay, especially considering the prices.  I guess you're paying for the view of the Bellagio fountain across the street.

Ah, this place only gets 3 stars.....it would get more but...read on:

1.  This is a great spot to eat after last call...packed, but damn it's good.  
2.  Always get it with Whiz....duh.
3.  Fries aren't half bad
4.  Be ready to cough up $10-12 for a sandwhich, fries, and a drink.

Okay...really....10-12 to eat at this place??????  Rip-off.  That' s my biggest complaint.  It would be different if the food was amazing or it was overflowing with meat....but it's neither of those things.

Food was ok but not spectacular.  Service was poor with our waitress (the other younger one seemed much nicer).  She had a poor attitude and got our order wrong.  We had the lo Mein noodle soup with chicken katsu.  And the Taiwanese pork dish.  Had asked for pork katsu with udon but we were hungry so stuck with it.

Chada Thai has been me and my husband's most favorite place in town for the last couple of months since we discovered this place. 

First, I love the atomospher of the restaurant. Good size, nice decorations and never be super crowded and loud so you can enjoy being there and having conversation with your friends, partners or whoever you are with. Good thing about here is I think you can come here for any pursose. While there are many couples, I also saw some people having business dinner on weekdays. 

The quality of food is absolutely fantastic! My favorite is PAD KRA-POW, YUM WOON SEN SEAFOOD and home made beef jerky. They are all amazing and I can eat this everyday. However, you have to be a little careful about choosing how spicy you want. I usually get between 5 to 7 out of 10 at Thai restaurants and when we first went there, I ordered "medium". Oh my got the food was very very spicy! I had probably 10 glasses of water until I finish my food but it was still good. When I asked the waiter if it was really medium, he said it was and he cannot eat medium too because it is too spicy. 

They have nice selections of wine too. I hear the owner of the restaurant used to be a　sommelier at Lotus of Siam, another favorite restaurant of us in Downtown who also have so many kinds of wine. 

Unfortunatelly I have not tried their desserts yet because we always end up feeling like having shaved ice nearby (Kuma!!) on the way home. Lol

The lunch specials are pretty average when compared to others around town. (I personally think the flavors and portions are bigger, with a smaller price tag, at nearby Thai Food To Go)  But I go there for their beautifully seasoned Eggplant with Fried Tofu.  They also have a great happy hour special which is available Mon-Fri from 4-6pm and all day on the weekends.  They offer almost any of their appetizers for $4 each.  This is a steal for their calamari (lightly breaded and perfectly tender) and perfectly crunchy and seasoned Thai dumplings.

This will be my third time in a row going to EDC!! The past two were prob the best weekends of my life! Im excited this year because I already bought my tix to get into the EDC Week Club scenes...last year we only could get into Tao one time...impossible for 5 guys to get into a club not on a VIP list!! But my group of ravers already have the pool party tix!! This year I paid a lot more for a tix to get into EDC then the last two years...not happy about that but oh well...its worth it!!! This years line up isnt as good as last years but I will be venturing out and seeing new sets from all dif types of EDM...I will DEF keep updating the review of things to check out! And adding a lot of pics! Vegas Baby!!!

I was wanting to find the pizza hut express to get one of those mini pizzas to take with me on the plane, but I didn't have the time to go look for it. I only had 50 minutes and I barely got there in time to line up with my section even though this was on the way. 

I ordered a slice of supreme even though I wanted pepperoni. The guy in front of me ordered the last three slices for his family. How annoying! 

Pros: 
-It was relatively quick and the pizza was edible. 

Cons: 
-Did not have more pepperoni or cheese pizza ready to go, but had plenty of the less popular flavors.
-Barely any pizza sauce on my pizza. 
-Crust thicker than I'd like it to be for NY style pizza.
-Pizza didn't seem as fresh and warm and other chains. 
-Expensive- almost $5 a slice. 
-Workers aren't that friendly.

Went here after finding it on yelp and it was everything people said. Cheap, Fast and friendly. Wish i could say the same about the DMV!, I Live in the far south anthem area but made the trip to this place. Id rather support a local business than those huge chains.

Pretty decent food - more towards the american menu. 

They do have a simple japanese sushi spread, not that fantastic. Oriental spread - pretty ok. American spread and italian spread(with pizzas and pastas) make up the bulk of their menu. 

Salad spread is good though, with different types of salads to choose and the buffet lady would start mixing the different greens together.

Drinks are free (any type), except the alcoholic ones.

Overall, I would give this a 6/10, definitely tasted way way better buffets than these, but it deserves the thumbs up for the price I paid for it and for the spread it serves.

Tip: One way to get really good and economical value buffets is to head to tix4dinner.com and get a buffet discount coupon. You pay 2 dollars and you get 25% or something like off your dinner buffet. Might be even more.

Once again, it isnt that fantastic but if you're on a budget, this should be more than sufficient!

Cheers!

This place doesn't even deserve one star!  It is absolutely disgusting!  Myself and my guest both got food poisoning from this place!  We went there on April 25, 2013.  Our sickness lasted for days!  Everything on the buffet was old, dried out and room temperature.  And omg....the bathrooms....wow!!!!  Absolutely disgusting!  The entire place was filthy.  I knew we should have walked out immediately!  Just from the smell inside the place.  Whatever you do.....remember....you are taking a very very big chance if you eat here!  I think it would be more likely than not....that you will end up sick!  We actually got staph food poisoning from here.  Otherwise known as \"Staphylococcus aureus bacterium\".  I truly believe this place needs to be SHUT DOWN PERMANENTLY!!!

The group went back to E2 today and the experience was much better. There were still some hits and misses with the food, but the service was on point. 

The good consisted of the BBQ shrimp, the potato soup, the roast beef po-boy and the fish sandwich. Also, the chocolate chip cookies were great. The bad was the pizza was undercooked, the gumbo was Ok, and the burger was undercooked. 

All in all, the meal was a vast improvement over last time. I am not sure if it was because they expected us and scrutinized our meal before giving it to us, or they made the necessary changes. 

I would recommend going and trying for yourself.

Lost baggage and poor child wrangling. It's all about employee empowerment.

I have the patience of the Madonna - all right maybe more like the Material Girl - but I am pretty laid back. After my humbling experience in the wine industry, I've been much more appreciative of service industry related jobs. But when employees are not empowered to answer the most simple of questions, and we're checking in on machines with nobody around to handle luggage, well...it's a disaster waiting to happen. It's an unfortunate thing that is supposed to help in keeping costs down and help with efficiency - but there's been consistent problems with all of my US AIRWAYS flights.

Let's throw JFK airport into the mix to help maximize the disaster...we ended up in Orlando. Waiting for our luggage. Luckily our toddler was entertained with the luggage roundabout...because we waited through two flight arrivals to retrieve one bag. 

Family that met up with us for the 4th assured us US AIRWAYS used a really efficient delivery service...Apparently the lost luggage situation is pretty common.

Good thing the baby bug is a trooper when it comes to traveling - because Orlando trips tend to be family-heavy and the on-board US AIRWAYS staff consistently doesn't seem too thrilled to be dealing with even the well-behaved ones.

First to review? 

Located next to Metro Pizza, this store has been around for years. Since there is no Fresh & Easy in my immediate area, I've had to find a replacement for my fresh produce. This location is super convenient to my workplace. It's hardly every busy (a few other customers, maybe a handful) so parking is easy, as well as checkout. Yesterday, I got packs of kiwi, peaches, a pkg. of celery, 3 pound bag of apples, a bunch of bananas for $12.05. I've been coming here more lately since F & E is closed and sometimes it's hit or miss with the 99 cents only store. 

Clean, fully stocked, and doesn't disappoint. I noticed two people working last night, one cashier, and one stocking.

I was pleasantly surprised by the excellent quality and value here. I got a $5.95 lunch special, which included a pizza slice, soda, and a nice-size salad. The pizza had a thin, crispy crust and was something called a red, white, and green--mozzarella, tomatoes, and spinach. Only complaint is the place is really small so table space is limited.

Maybe we went here on an off day (eventhough the place was relatively empty) but me and my foodie cohorts were not really impressed with the food that we were served during our visit here.

First let me say that the decor was impressive(Why that's important to me, I'll never know...)  A completely different take on the space from the previous Taste of Dahntahn and  much appreciated.

Okay, here are my thoughts on the food.... I was kind of disappointed with my food.  I am a beef brisket addict and so is one of my co-workers.  We both ordered the smoked brisket sandwich (I actually ordered the sandwich as a part of the G &G Power Lunch, along with the small carrot salad.) and my other co-worker ordered the the Roasted Pork Ramen. The beef brisket was not tender and actually chewy, with a greasy aftertaste that was not pleasant.  It was almost as though the beef had been made quite some time before we got there and sat in beef fat until the taste became less beefy and more stale tasting.  The carrot salad was really nothing to write home about either, however, it was attractive looking. (And I mean that in good way...)  I was very surprised at the lack of available side dishes too.

My co-worker who ordered the Roasted Pork Ramen made out like a fat rat!  The broth was delicious and the serving size was decent.  

Overall, it was okay.  The food was less impressive than I expected and the decor was better than I expected.  Additionally, the service was respectable and the wait staff knowledgeble.  All things considered, this adds up to an "A-Ok" rating versus a "Meh, I've experienced better"

I filled my ample belly with OJ, fresh cantaloupe, ambrosia, easy over eggs, corned beef hash and a tiny spoonful of bread pudding.  Overall, I felt this buffet has slipped a notch since our last visit.

One of the very best restaurants in Vegas. A true hidden gem!

Why go to Le Circ or Bouchon and pay overinflated prices for a fine French meal, when you can go to Pamplemousse for half the price and have a true French experience?

It doesn't look like much from the street, but walk inside and you will be instantly transported to Paris.

The food is unrivaled IMHO. I highly recommend the Fettucine ala George, a marvelously tasty appetizer as is the lobster bisque. They handle fish selections wonderfully and the tournedos are incredible!

Anytime we are in Vegas we always dine here, don't miss it, it's wonderful, and easily half the price of any other French restaurant in Vegas you might consider.
Wings are overpriced. And the quality of them are bad. They were tough and greasy. The staff are pleasant but then over all experience was too expensive for a sports bar.

The food was great. The service was terrible.  Waited 30 minutes for our reservation. Finally had to ask then waited 10 minutes to pay the bill finally went up to the front and paid the bill. It is really noisy   If you are looking for quiet and nice look elsewhere
I love shanghai-nese food since I am shanghai-nese.  The staff was helpful in ordering stuff.  Food was quite good so no complaints.  I would come back when I am in town.

Home away from home. Great selection of Cigars, wrist watches and shaving products. Great place to hang out and have a smoke and watch sports on 6 large tv screens. No membership fees and you can also store you collection in the back room free of charge. Highly recommend Cigar King.
Pretty good gyros. Not the best I've ever had but you won't be disappointed. Just a bit pricey. .. Probably because of lack of traffic they have to make up for it.

I've been taking my cats to Dr. Steelman for 15 years.  I was always a huge fan of her work in the community.  I am deeply disappointed in my last dealings with that place and Dr. Steelman. My cat was very ill, and you NEVER can reach anyone there. They NEVER return your phone calls. You wait HOURS to pick them up.  Then when you do, if your cat is still sick, and you have questions, THEY STILL DON'T return your calls. HOW SCARY. The receptionist keeps saying we are VERY busy. SO is the VA hospital and people DIE!     If you are VERY BUSY then our poor family members get forgotten.  Vodka Ghilarducci (my 15 year old purr master) pasted away today 5/24/14 of fluid in the lungs and heart.  Rainbow Animal Hospital was able to detect his pain and laboring breathing that was so hard to watch. We chose to do the right thing. I will NEVER GO BACK TO THAT PLACE. After all as they say, \"WE ARE VERY VERY BUSY\"!!!!!  Keep that in mind when you bring your babies there!\n\nHere is THE CLINC'S MISSION STATEMENT:  WE UNDERSTAND THAT YOUR CAT IS AN IMPORTANT MEMBER OF THE FAMILY.    How is this EVEN possible when you DON'T call your clients back? How is this possible when you admit to being TOO busy?   Simple common sense say NOT!

oh my gosh. this drink got me drunkk in like 5 minutes no joke.

its pretty similar to fat tuesday but when its a million degrees outside, you really dont feel like walking that far down the strip to get it..especially if youre staying closer to the north end of the strip. and plus lime ice's bottles are way cuter!

anywho. i got the pink lemonade 22oz for like $12. what a deal. in goes the shots of bacardi... and the slushee.. and you've got yourself a party! they mix it for you but somehow all the alcohol settles to the bottom, so the first 5-7 sips are so strong they just get you buzzinnnn. great way to beat the heat and get drunk at the same time. whats not to love!

I go to the Delhi Cafe about once a week - I can't resist! I love everything about it (apart from the toilet facilities). 

The food is cheap and delicious. The curry is very flavourful, the naan bread just the right amount of garlicky, the rice is lovely and a refreshing little side of fresh salad and couscous comes on your plate. Portions are the perfect size, enough to not make you feel like you'd want more, but not so much that it's a struggle.  Yummy, comforting food that doesn't leave you feeling over-stuffed.

The staff are lovely as well, very efficient and up for a wee chat. 

What more could you want? Love it! (I'd give it 5 stars if they gave the toilet a bit of a re-furb.)

I wish I continued reading more reviews instead of just the first 3 and looking at the pictures.
